name: Rural Americans United
subject: ICYMI: The First Real Threat to Citizens United Is Coming from Montana
body:
While Congress stalls and courts enable overreach, one state may have uncovered the path to restore democratic control.
| | | | |
---
| What Is In This Newsletter?

* Montana Steps Up Against Citizens United
* Progressive Strategies NW Data: Hispanic Voter Turnout
* Twelve Billion Dollar Bailout, What a Buffoon

---
| |
---
| Have we finally found a way to deal a death blow to Citizens United? With the Supreme Court handing out unprecedented powers to the Executive branch like cake at a birthday party, and our Democratic members of Congress offering little more than strong rebukes, it often feels hopeless to reclaim lost ground. But while MAGA twists the rule of law into an unrecognizable pretzel, [Montana](https://zmhksxhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wxWwOe4tzBnkTWfopkemOupnnyF9uyUQ0dcd2_ouJwbeMe7a5TMDBKQqMcaR1bdfoC6RLhnkgOHuX646QIoqQdyQ3NVBsQwYtOvi1O-GTalvhDdpavMptzzkV5HYVu1KE3QJbyATPy5QMmeU5VcWTZT-mNLCWyJzaPfo4GmGWws=&c=5L16xAtk5GawJc7Mh5a4YVkm6_ptxEcsZFj5Tlgx_p3S-nFXEfMPig==&ch=rN4daB7ca1ksJ0oBmil3F6QaQVlz__OS9-A-gA0pyTzqkT0YeiZyog==) may have found a way to put real teeth into trimming back the rampant oligarchic control of our government.
Could it really be that easy? Anyone who has ever started a business knows that the first step is registering with a state. It does not have to be the state where you live, but it is at the state level that every business in the United States begins. Whether a single owner, partnership, LLC, non profit, or PAC, it all starts with state registration. From there, everyone must answer to the IRS, but depending on the type of business, additional federal filings may be required, such as with the Securities and Exchange Commission for finance or the Federal Election Commission for partisan federal elections. It is state authority that governs almost all of the rights and powers granted to a business. And this state authority is the key to undoing Citizens United.
In Montana, of all places, this idea has taken hold under the name [Transparent Election Initiative](https://zmhksxhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wxWwOe4tzBnkTWfopkemOupnnyF9uyUQ0dcd2_ouJwbeMe7a5TMDBKQqMcaR1bdfoC6RLhnkgOHuX646QIoqQdyQ3NVBsQwYtOvi1O-GTalvhDdpavMptzzkV5HYVu1KE3QJbyATPy5QMmeU5VcWTVkHUzFGgVWrpRnoNGw5s6c=&c=5L16xAtk5GawJc7Mh5a4YVkm6_ptxEcsZFj5Tlgx_p3S-nFXEfMPig==&ch=rN4daB7ca1ksJ0oBmil3F6QaQVlz__OS9-A-gA0pyTzqkT0YeiZyog==). The initiative is so clean in its design that it would be nearly impossible even for our MAGA friendly Supreme Court to overturn. [Harvard Law](https://zmhksxhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wxWwOe4tzBnkTWfopkemOupnnyF9uyUQ0dcd2_ouJwbeMe7a5TMDBKQqMcaR1bdfsqM7pMQJ3yB_S4egbsKHJ1j-wJZqtPuEi6Q4TS3ecXiixp8c5L3Mf39_b41WKxOaEVuNGeFDhvJnOw3kWtrbW9o9N5Fg_21BLnGEPq9t4grZxB0iehaKZJ-GdomXAHqi8Y9vIMNzA4OTu_7Yk1bG603Rn3jW2Ub-3ZVx6ovHjII=&c=5L16xAtk5GawJc7Mh5a4YVkm6_ptxEcsZFj5Tlgx_p3S-nFXEfMPig==&ch=rN4daB7ca1ksJ0oBmil3F6QaQVlz__OS9-A-gA0pyTzqkT0YeiZyog==) weighed in on the subject. States rights in corporate governance are reinforced by hundreds of years of legal precedent. For the Supreme Court to overturn that doctrine would completely upend virtually every aspect of commerce and finance in this country. A real debacle. Of course, [opposition exists](https://zmhksxhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wxWwOe4tzBnkTWfopkemOupnnyF9uyUQ0dcd2_ouJwbeMe7a5TMDBKQqMcaR1bdfeXQt4QHi1YvaM_JfuLzAwp07gRG-p4MS1GhAwZxHuh1AfsVymttpdeZw-bxkyETFq4Qz5Gps7H8W7EDjiavl8kqFHlahd1Rd-Z2WrOiTwAkL8JBfMmjusDsuePH_9gX0j19fUZ6dF-BgNpaR2poZkPu-DXriANb6YLwG5CyNGmui6x5YXXQdk1Jlqr_FsmmaIB422MvPeKM=&c=5L16xAtk5GawJc7Mh5a4YVkm6_ptxEcsZFj5Tlgx_p3S-nFXEfMPig==&ch=rN4daB7ca1ksJ0oBmil3F6QaQVlz__OS9-A-gA0pyTzqkT0YeiZyog==), but it focuses on specific wording in the initiative, not the underlying concept.

---
| |
---
|
Is there a downside? Yes, and it mostly affects 501(c)(4) non profits. This designation allows non profits to engage in political activity, including spending up to forty nine percent of their budget on partisan politics. They can directly tell the public to vote for or against a candidate or a ballot initiative. Under the Transparent Election Initiative, 501(c)(4) corporations would lose the right to engage in partisan political activity. So where does that leave us? Surprisingly, with more room than it first appears.
Political Action Committees are also corporations registered with the state, and therefore subject to state governance. In the Montana version of the Transparent Election Initiative, PACs are exempted, meaning they would retain the ability to engage in partisan politics, though without a tax exemption. Also, the line between partisan and non partisan political speech remains wide and usable.
In practice, what you can say in advertising is only lightly restricted. It is far more likely that media platforms such as Facebook, radio, television, or newspapers will act as stronger censors than the Federal Election Commission, which oversees federal political messaging by 501(c)(4) organizations.
If there is still a large gap in keeping big money out of partisan politics, is the Transparent Election Initiative still worth pursuing? Absolutely. It restricts businesses that exist for commerce to activities consistent with that purpose. And if an organization wants to engage in politics, it requires a separate corporate structure created specifically for that purpose, which increases transparency.

---
| |
---
| An example of a non-partisan educational message. This billboard in Fairfield is one of nine billboards in Congressional District 5 hosted by Help Save America/ [Prosperity Eastern Washington](https://zmhksxhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wxWwOe4tzBnkTWfopkemOupnnyF9uyUQ0dcd2_ouJwbeMe7a5TMDBKQqMcaR1bdfm4NuVio3IKsLxg0t2wdeDDOg-5tn-lJJrQXMqZvNsJQV9dlVsF3PjVwsvHfkbn4PmDehe167FeVJ37PnI9Xge-MnIN-oj_SV&c=5L16xAtk5GawJc7Mh5a4YVkm6_ptxEcsZFj5Tlgx_p3S-nFXEfMPig==&ch=rN4daB7ca1ksJ0oBmil3F6QaQVlz__OS9-A-gA0pyTzqkT0YeiZyog==)
If there is still a large gap in keeping big money out of partisan politics, is the Transparent Election Initiative still worth pursuing? Absolutely. It restricts businesses that exist for commerce to activities consistent with that purpose. And if an organization wants to engage in politics, it requires a separate corporate structure created specifically for that purpose, which increases transparency.

| |

-Text and data provided by Progressive Strategies NW. Text has been edited to fit.
In the 2025 General Election, turnout struggled most in Franklin and Yakima counties \-- also Washington State's most Hispanic districts.
That's not a coincidence. Especially in local elections, turnout struggles significantly among Hispanic voters. Estimates using voter names and location -- a statistical model of likely voter race -- indicate that only 14.5% of likely Hispanic voters returned a ballot, compared to 36.7% overall. Likely Native American voters also had very low turnout (17.5%). While turnout was higher among identified African-American (21.9%) and Asian (23.0%), it was much less than voters modeled as likely Caucasian (40.3%).
This aligns with broader trends in local elections across the country. While socioeconomic factors like income certainly play a role, they are deeply intertwined with race, and race remains a distinct driver of turnout on its own. We see repeatedly that 'participation gaps' widen in off-cycle elections specifically for communities of color, regardless of other variables. These communities stand the most to gain electorally from fostering greater civic engagement.

---
|
---
This is why the work of Rural Americans United is essential. When turnout collapses among Hispanic, and Native communities, the political power of rural Washington collapses with it, leaving our democracy less representative and the most affected voices pushed further to the margins. Rural Americans United exists to reverse that trend through voter education, targeted outreach, and pro democracy communication that narrows the participation gap and ensures every rural voter is seen, heard, and empowered.

During 47's first administration I was living in Hong Kong, China. While he was boasting about bringing China to its knees with his trade war, I watched China laugh all the way to the bank as the United States ran up the largest trade imbalance in recent history with China. That trade war was so devastating to American farmers that twenty three billion dollars in bailout money was spent to offset farming losses.

| This time around, 47, in true MAGA style, doubled down on the disaster. Instead of losing only to China, we have now managed to lose to the world. When 47 attacked all countries, they collectively responded with a global economic reset that excluded the United States. It is questionable whether the United States can ever fully recover.
When our main trading partners, China, Canada, and Mexico, secured markets outside the United States for trade, they placed themselves in a far stronger economic position. They no longer have to worry about the petulant and rancid tirades of a man who behaves erratically, and they do not have to consider the moral implications of trading with a country that so easily abuses its citizens and ignores established laws and conventions.
How specifically does Washington lose? Not all farmers in Washington will miss out. The United States Department of Agriculture has published a list of crops that are eligible for a one time payment. What is again blatantly obvious is the absence of fruit farmers. Do Nothing Dan failed to move the Farm Bill forward, which opened the door for it to be gutted by the Big Beautiful Bill. He has supported all of 47's disastrous economic decisions, and not once has he stood up to ensure that fruit ranchers are included in the framework of agricultural support.
Rural Americans United is working to counter this failure by amplifying rural voices, exposing the harm these policies cause, and delivering pro democracy messaging that holds leaders accountable to the communities they ignore.

clean_body: While Congress stalls and courts enable overreach, one state may have uncovered the path to restore democratic control. | | | | | | What Is In This Newsletter? Montana Steps Up Against Citizens United Progressive Strategies NW Data: Hispanic Voter Turnout * Twelve Billion Dollar Bailout, What a Buffoon | | | Have we finally found a way to deal a death blow to Citizens United? With the Supreme Court handing out unprecedented powers to the Executive branch like cake at a birthday party, and our Democratic members of Congress offering little more than strong rebukes, it often feels hopeless to reclaim lost ground. But while MAGA twists the rule of law into an unrecognizable pretzel, == may have found a way to put real teeth into trimming back the rampant oligarchic control of our government. Could it really be that easy? Anyone who has ever started a business knows that the first step is registering with a state. It does not have to be the state where you live, but it is at the state level that every business in the United States begins. Whether a single owner, partnership, LLC, non profit, or PAC, it all starts with state registration. From there, everyone must answer to the IRS, but depending on the type of business, additional federal filings may be required, such as with the Securities and Exchange Commission for finance or the Federal Election Commission for partisan federal elections. It is state authority that governs almost all of the rights and powers granted to a business. And this state authority is the key to undoing Citizens United. In Montana, of all places, this idea has taken hold under the name ==. The initiative is so clean in its design that it would be nearly impossible even for our MAGA friendly Supreme Court to overturn. == weighed in on the subject. States rights in corporate governance are reinforced by hundreds of years of legal precedent. For the Supreme Court to overturn that doctrine would completely upend virtually every aspect of commerce and finance in this country. A real debacle. Of course, ==, but it focuses on specific wording in the initiative, not the underlying concept. | | | Is there a downside? Yes, and it mostly affects 501(c)(4) non profits. This designation allows non profits to engage in political activity, including spending up to forty nine percent of their budget on partisan politics. They can directly tell the public to vote for or against a candidate or a ballot initiative. Under the Transparent Election Initiative, 501(c)(4) corporations would lose the right to engage in partisan political activity. So where does that leave us? Surprisingly, with more room than it first appears. Political Action Committees are also corporations registered with the state, and therefore subject to state governance. In the Montana version of the Transparent Election Initiative, PACs are exempted, meaning they would retain the ability to engage in partisan politics, though without a tax exemption. Also, the line between partisan and non partisan political speech remains wide and usable. In practice, what you can say in advertising is only lightly restricted. It is far more likely that media platforms such as Facebook, radio, television, or newspapers will act as stronger censors than the Federal Election Commission, which oversees federal political messaging by 501(c)(4) organizations. If there is still a large gap in keeping big money out of partisan politics, is the Transparent Election Initiative still worth pursuing? Absolutely. It restricts businesses that exist for commerce to activities consistent with that purpose. And if an organization wants to engage in politics, it requires a separate corporate structure created specifically for that purpose, which increases transparency. | | | An example of a non-partisan educational message. This billboard in Fairfield is one of nine billboards in Congressional District 5 hosted by Help Save America/ == If there is still a large gap in keeping big money out of partisan politics, is the Transparent Election Initiative still worth pursuing? Absolutely. It restricts businesses that exist for commerce to activities consistent with that purpose. And if an organization wants to engage in politics, it requires a separate corporate structure created specifically for that purpose, which increases transparency. | | -Text and data provided by Progressive Strategies NW. Text has been edited to fit. In the 2025 General Election, turnout struggled most in Franklin and Yakima counties \-- also Washington State's most Hispanic districts. That's not a coincidence. Especially in local elections, turnout struggles significantly among Hispanic voters. Estimates using voter names and location -- a statistical model of likely voter race -- indicate that only 14.5% of likely Hispanic voters returned a ballot, compared to 36.7% overall. Likely Native American voters also had very low turnout (17.5%). While turnout was higher among identified African-American (21.9%) and Asian (23.0%), it was much less than voters modeled as likely Caucasian (40.3%). This aligns with broader trends in local elections across the country. While socioeconomic factors like income certainly play a role, they are deeply intertwined with race, and race remains a distinct driver of turnout on its own. We see repeatedly that 'participation gaps' widen in off-cycle elections specifically for communities of color, regardless of other variables. These communities stand the most to gain electorally from fostering greater civic engagement. | This is why the work of Rural Americans United is essential. When turnout collapses among Hispanic, and Native communities, the political power of rural Washington collapses with it, leaving our democracy less representative and the most affected voices pushed further to the margins. Rural Americans United exists to reverse that trend through voter education, targeted outreach, and pro democracy communication that narrows the participation gap and ensures every rural voter is seen, heard, and empowered. | == | Twelve Billion Dollar Bailout, What a Buffoon. Instead of losing only to China, we have now managed to lose to the world. When 47 attacked all countries, they collectively responded with a global economic reset that excluded the United States. It is questionable whether the United States can ever fully recover. Dear USO Supporter,
Sometimes, our service members need a hero -- and sometimes these heroes happen to have four legs and a tail.
When the stresses of deployment and being far from home are especially difficult for our service members, the USO Canine Program steps in with all four paws. This highly requested program is especially critical during the holiday season, when being far from loved ones and beloved traditions is even more difficult.
**USO Canine Volunteers provide emotional and mental health support, visit injured or sick service members at Warrior and Family Centers and provide comfort to military children who have lost a parent. Their visits alone are associated with lowering cases of PTSD, decreasing anxiety and helping with depression.**
**[This Christmas, give the gift of more dog pets and other critical services to those bravely serving our country. Remember, thanks to the generosity of our friends at FedEx, your gift DOUBLES, dollar for dollar, up to $100,000.](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/ZdvWh04N-3ty/cTBsZHpONjhSTGtFR3NMK2RMOVVXRGpyTERyL2c2emY1S2lWSEM5Smg0K1pPTHc2SisrUTlGVlVXakJuRUZKUVdxRExYTW9DUEJINUpVUlhGKzgwWk9xbmZVTnUyUHJsclZyMk9jNVNMb3M9S0/a2NkSGQvUjNBRkhkU2kzUm5RTTI1YnpJQXVqWjBPbVZwTU1KSWxlRitUc0p1ZmgrMnhjRVdma2xUMk9LSFJQaEhRNHVCMFRva1V2R0ZuR1JGa0FWUHRsWnZHU09CeTlBS0)**

name: GOTV PAC
subject: Democrats Are Winning, but why is the GOP likely to keep the House?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09042025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09042025&amount=25]
Peter ,
Democrats have been outperforming in special elections across the country. We’ve flipped seats, closed margins, and proven voters are rejecting MAGA extremism.
But here’s the truth we can’t ignore: hoping a blue wave naturally happens on its own won't save us in 2026. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email12142025]
Just this month, the Supreme Court cleared the way for Texas Republicans to move forward with a new congressional map that could hand the GOP five additional seats – despite evidence of racial gerrymandering.
And Texas is just the start.
At Donald Trump’s urging, Republicans are pushing aggressive redistricting efforts nationwide – from Florida to Missouri and beyond – designed to lock in House control before a single vote is cast.
Democrats are fighting back where we can, but Republicans control more state legislatures and face fewer legal barriers. The result? A House battlefield tilted against voters before Election Day even arrives.
That’s why assuming a “blue wave” is dangerous.
Winning in 2026 will require overwhelming turnout – strong enough to beat gerrymandered maps and voter suppression.
That’s where GOTV PAC comes in. We invest early in organizing and turnout infrastructure that can overpower unfair maps with real votes.
Can you chip in today to help GOTV PAC scale up for 2026? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email12142025]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

If we prepare early, we can still take back the House.
GOTV PAC

Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]

This petition is registered to you only.

Donald Trump only became President in 2016 because of the outdated Electoral College.

_Hillary Clinton won the popular vote by nearly 2.9 million. But the Electoral College overruled her popular vote victory._

**Now, Sen. Elizabeth Warren is urgently pleading with Democrats:**
We couldn't agree more. But now that Trump is in office, he and his Republican cronies will do everything in their power to uphold the Electoral College.

**So we need to act now -- because we must prevent unpopular, unqualified candidates from EVER becoming President again.**

[**_If every Democrat reading this signs our petition, we will reach our 125,OOO signature goal within minutes and gain traction for this urgent legislation in Congress. Please add your name immediately:_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71008?t=5&akid=23726%2E4449941%2Ev2sYWB)

It’s been a big year.

In the Senate, we’ve delivered President Trump’s agenda, cut your taxes, and secured our border.

**I’ve officially filed to run for re-election and I’m proud to be endorsed by President Trump, Governor Sarah Sanders, and conservative leaders across the country. **

clean_body: Defending our values in the Senate| | | It’s been a big year. In the Senate, we’ve delivered President Trump’s agenda, cut your taxes, and secured our border. I’ve officially filed to run for re-election and I’m proud to be endorsed by President Trump, Governor Sarah Sanders, and conservative leaders across the country. We’re in for a fight come 2026. Democrats have their eyes on Arkansas. They’re running the same playbook they always do: Pick a far-left fraud and send their campaign millions to dupe voters about their record. There’s no path to keeping the Senate Majority without keeping Arkansas red. So that’s why it’s so important we earn as many grassroots endorsements as we can, before the attack ads even get started. Thank you for standing with me before in defense of our conservative values and the fight to make America strong again. In service, Tom Cotton | | Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army. Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any Service Branch. Friends, Tonight, millions of people around the world will begin an 8-day celebration of resilience, tenacity, and hope. For the Jewish people, Hanukkah is a time to gather with friends and family, celebrate, and reflect on the past year. **Hanukkah offers a key message: light will always overcome darkness.** I hope that in the next eight days, you have time to reflect on this past year, gather with your community, and be reminded that hope always overcomes fear and love will always triumph over hate. To those who celebrate, Chag Sameach, Happy Holiday, from my family to yours! \- Marlene Galán-Woods

subject: Pizza Power and The Economy

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/cf30b209-a624-4a1f-95bc-573a7ea15749?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Earlier this year, I wrote a short piece about pizza's power to predict major domestic and geopolitical events, noting that unusually large late night pizza order delivered to the Pentagon, White House, or even Congress had been correlated with major geopolitical developments emerging from Washington DC. After all, during an emerging or ongoing crisis, professionals involved in the planning or execution of a response or military action are probably stuck in a secure location and pizza becomes the fuel to power the team through the tense night. Nice theory, but does it work?
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..2IamEoNKx9zueUoWFIn39z9MWyg-aIkQVS0cyUB2hqk?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=181620417)
The short answer is yes. Turns out that pizza deliveries boomed during the nights before the First Gulf War began, the Clinton impeachment, the May 2011 raid on Osama Bin Laden, and even Israel's attack on Gaza. Sure seems that unexpected surges in pizza deliveries to the secure offices of important decision-makers are an early signal of something big about to happen.
* * *
## [Pizza's Power to Predict](https://substack.com/redirect/8abb912a-ac42-477d-bcf6-71416cab65ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Jun 21
[](https://substack.com/redirect/8abb912a-ac42-477d-bcf6-71416cab65ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Recently, I've been intensely focused on all things pizza - a natural consequence of my having recently started a frozen pizza manufacturing business. Pizza news, the pizza market, anything affecting the pizza ingredient supply chain...all grab my attention. And I always detour to the frozen section of any grocery store to see the offerings, placement, an...](https://substack.com/redirect/8abb912a-ac42-477d-bcf6-71416cab65ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/8abb912a-ac42-477d-bcf6-71416cab65ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
As someone who has advised leaders to navigate global uncertainty by connecting developments across seemingly disparate or disconnected domains, the relationship between pizza and world-shaking events naturally caught my attention. But the more I think about it, the more I realize that pizza's usefulness as an indicator probably isn't constrained to just political developments.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..2IamEoNKx9zueUoWFIn39z9MWyg-aIkQVS0cyUB2hqk?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=181620417)
After all, who doesn't like pizza? It's a quick meal for kids (or adults) when time's tight, the perfect food while hosting friends, and one of the easiest and most cost-effective ways to feed workers during a meeting. Given that the average American eats [180 slices a year](https://substack.com/redirect/f3451b2c-379c-485b-8fb9-fdcc27a39a35?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), pizza consumption might be a unique seismograph capable of reflecting ...

name: Jena Griswold
subject: Happy Hanukkah!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2512-q4-jg?refcode=251214-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2512-q4-jg?refcode=251214-email]
Peter -
As families across Colorado and around the world gather to light the menorah, I want to wish everyone celebrating a warm and joyful Hanukkah.
This holiday reminds us of the power of resilience, hope, and community – that even in the darkest moments, a small light can grow brighter and guide the way forward. It’s a message that feels especially meaningful right now, as so many are working tirelessly to protect our rights, strengthen our democracy, and ensure every community feels safe and valued.
To all who are celebrating, I hope these eight nights bring you peace, connection, and moments of joy with the people you love.
Chag Sameach - and may your Hanukkah be filled with light!
Warmly,
Jena

body:
We can't stress this enough: For less than the price of a latte at your local coffee shop, you can help cover the costs of registering a new voter.
We have to start now to be ready for next year's midterm elections. RUSH $24 or more today and help us register a tidal wave of new voters nationwide >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?refcode=121425_2&amount=24]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Our voter registration work doesn't end, folks.
Despite vote-suppressing state legislatures making it harder to register to vote -- passing bills to stop organizations like ours and our partners on the ground from helping potential voters navigate the increasingly murky rules around voter registration -- we're still pulling out all the stops to bring voters into the system.
Voter registration is a critical part of grassroots efforts to protect voting rights. The more people who vote, the more our government looks like we, the people. But to get to the point where every voice is heard, we need your help .
Just $3 can help us register a new voter. Will you chip in $24 or more today to help register 8 new voters? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?refcode=121425_2&amount=24]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121425_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121425_2&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121425_2&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121425_2&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121425_2&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121425_2&amount=36]
Voter participation has been increasing, and young people -- a major target of opponents of voting rights -- see the importance of voting now more than ever. Voting quickly becomes a habit -- a habit our democracy sorely needs to fulfill the promise of the American dream. Overwhelming key states with new voter registrations can help turn the tide against the voter suppression laws we've seen passed the last few years.
The next election will determine whether our democracy stays mired in the darkness and conspiracy theories we hear every day or if we can reclaim our rights -- from voting to choice and more -- and march forward to a better future.
New voter registration -- at just about $3 per new voter -- can make a huge difference. Please, can you pitch in with $24 or whatever you can to help us register a wave of new voters ahead of this year's registration deadlines? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?refcode=121425_2&amount=24]
Thank you,
Voter Action Project's Registration Team
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121425_2&amount=20]

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=vfilQghBNhhn5rOBeezyU5Vn2waBsiPAV3N20L2SDwfZP1VSBveIozpAvTvWUFYLQzKoyqi8ZFWYk6Ey9v4Z56tD%2FaUArfuLHXE6TPF0iQrCgXd8eUavilX4NqC1K95ruXCQLb8HIcb09eVM6Z%2B1F26QXE%2FiAQdWuBCUnaMCqkGa7x2BSDfMMBxaVpMNTnzGznoewMCbUYDzh4zxRa2xXa4K0YzjeY%2FTSe9SShoZuOQyGPQGiXdVSnHCrUVZtns0xC1vBI5XLa6TzECv19Dj12xB%2FAewYuJLymVG00EWQHuwUT%2FXlH%2B41KgYjywI9uafnjf45Q0c8eVJuSt2tYt89cbk4Y6MoWQesP6Zv81Y2mjW4Q29lglv9YYda1E%2FcTLVzXbkeYIfjPwiL1eaVvlDbvE1%2FKz%2FcsHyFQZDw4sNKS8%3D .
clean_body: We can't stress this enough: For less than the price of a latte at your local coffee shop, you can help cover the costs of registering a new voter. We have to start now to be ready for next year's midterm elections. RUSH $24 or more today and help us register a tidal wave of new voters nationwide >> [] Voter Action Project [] Our voter registration work doesn't end, folks. Despite vote-suppressing state legislatures making it harder to register to vote -- passing bills to stop organizations like ours and our partners on the ground from helping potential voters navigate the increasingly murky rules around voter registration -- we're still pulling out all the stops to bring voters into the system. Voter registration is a critical part of grassroots efforts to protect voting rights. The more people who vote, the more our government looks like we, the people. But to get to the point where every voice is heard, we need your help . Just $3 can help us register a new voter. Will you chip in $24 or more today to help register 8 new voters? >> [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [] OTHER AMOUNT [] Voter participation has been increasing, and young people -- a major target of opponents of voting rights -- see the importance of voting now more than ever. Voting quickly becomes a habit -- a habit our democracy sorely needs to fulfill the promise of the American dream. Overwhelming key states with new voter registrations can help turn the tide against the voter suppression laws we've seen passed the last few years. The next election will determine whether our democracy stays mired in the darkness and conspiracy theories we hear every day or if we can reclaim our rights -- from voting to choice and more -- and march forward to a better future. New voter registration -- at just about $3 per new voter -- can make a huge difference. Please, can you pitch in with $24 or whatever you can to help us register a wave of new voters ahead of this year's registration deadlines? >> [] Thank you, Voter Action Project's Registration Team Voter Action Project [] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [] Donate Now [] Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003. Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Integrity First Voter Action Project P.O. name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 12/14/2025
body:
The Tina Peters story took another turn this week — here’s the clear, complete update
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**December 14, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST**
| | Dear Rocky Mountain Voice family,
It was a _*Trump voice*_ HUGE week of news in Colorado. Such a big week that our website crashed a couple of times due to crazy amounts of traffic, thanks for that! _We encourage you to read this edition in full and take time with our top articles below — this is the kind of news you don’t want to fall behind on._
This week, no story came close to the reach or impact of the **Tina Peters case**. What began years ago as a local prosecution has now become one of the most closely watched election-integrity and civil-rights stories in the country — and our reporting helped push it into the national spotlight.
**How This Case Began** Tina Peters is the former elected clerk of Mesa County, responsible for administering elections — including federal elections — under both state and federal law. In 2021, she authorized the preservation of election system records ahead of a scheduled software update. Peters has consistently maintained that she did so to comply with **federal law** , which requires election officials to preserve records tied to federal elections for 22 months.
At trial, jurors were **[not allowed to hear that federal-law defense](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**. Peters was convicted in state court and sentenced to nine years in prison. That unresolved conflict — between state prosecution and federal election law — is the foundation of this case.
**What Changed This Week** Several major developments collided at once.
Colorado quietly released — then heavily redacted — a letter from the U.S. Bureau of Prisons requesting that Peters be transferred to federal custody. An [unredacted version](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) reviewed by Rocky Mountain Voice shows federal officials cited concerns about her confinement conditions and confirmed she has **no federal charges**. Nearly all of that explanation was removed before the letter was made public.
On the same day a federal judge declined to intervene in Peters’ habeas petition — while acknowledging that her case raises “important constitutional questions” — she was placed into isolation confinement for 22 hours a day. That move came just as the U.S. Department of Justice announced a [civil-rights investigation](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) into conditions inside Colorado’s prison system, including the facility where Peters is held.
Days later, President Trump announced a **[formal Executive Grant of Clemency](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** , granting Peters a full and unconditional pardon for offenses related to election integrity and security during the 2020–2021 period. This was not a symbolic gesture — it was a signed pardon issued under Article II of the Constitution and transmitted through the Department of Justice.
Peters’ legal team immediately notified the Colorado Department of Corrections and arrived at the prison expecting her release. _Colorado refused._
**Why the Pardon Is Being Disputed** Colorado’s position is straightforward: presidential pardons apply to **federal crimes** , not state convictions. From the state’s perspective, Peters is a state inmate serving a state sentence, and the pardon has no effect.
But Peters’ attorneys argue the case is not that simple.
When local officials administer **federal elections** , they do so under **federal authority** , bound first by federal law. The U.S. Supreme Court has long held that election officials conducting federal elections operate under federal oversight.
If Peters was prosecuted for actions taken to comply with federal election-record preservation laws — and that defense was never allowed before a jury — her attorneys argue the case falls within the President’s constitutional pardon power for “offenses against the United States.”
That question has **never been answered by a court**.
**Why This Matters Beyond One Case** This is no longer just about Tina Peters. It’s about whether states can punish election officials for complying with federal law — and then block federal review by labeling prosecutions “state-only." It’s about whether speech on election vulnerabilities can be used to justify harsher sentencing. And it’s about transparency, due process, and whether political narratives have replaced constitutional scrutiny.
**What Happens Next** Peters remains in Colorado custody while her state appeal continues. Federal courts have acknowledged serious constitutional issues but have so far declined to intervene. Colorado has rejected both federal concern and a presidential pardon.
The unresolved questions now move toward higher courts — and possibly the U.S. Supreme Court — where the clash between state authority and federal election law may finally be addressed.
Until then, this case remains unfinished — and deeply consequential. And we will continue to follow it, report it, and explain it clearly — because Coloradans deserve to understand what’s happening in their own state, without filters, slogans, or selective silence.
— Team RMV
---
| | |
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[What Colorado redacted from a federal prison letter—and why it matters](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The state confirmed it received a request from the Bureau of Prisons regarding the transfer of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters to federal custody. What the public has not been allowed to see is why the federal government made it in the first place.
**[Tina Peters Requests Presidential Pardon as New Evidence Bolsters Her Claims](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY A.L. GOODWIN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) On December 6, 2025, former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters formally submitted an application for a Presidential Pardon to President Donald J. Trump. Her legal team describes the request as both a matter of justice and national security, pointing to newly released evidence and expanding federal investigations that directly corroborate Peters’ original claims.
**[A Town on Edge: Inside the Erie Mail Threats That Resulted in No Charges](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) In early October, residents across Erie and in Virginia, began opening their mailboxes to find sympathy cards inside plain envelopes. The message referenced the assassination of Charlie Kirk, accompanied by an empty packet of Gushers candy taped to the card, glitter and a loose powdery substance that spilled when handled.

**STATE**
**[Colorado Progressive Tax Plan Advances Seeking End to Flat Tax and TABOR Limits](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COMPLETE COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) After years of voter-approved measures scaling back Colorado’s income tax rate, proponents of significantly higher government spending have cleared a major hurdle at the state Title Board towards raising taxes by $4.1 billion annually.

**[Caught between two governments: Whistleblower says Colorado’s DEI system collides with a federal ban](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When managers in the Colorado Department of Agriculture (CDA) logged into their Nov. 6 virtual meeting, the agenda read like a standard operational update. But on the list sat two items that created angst: the statewide EDI/Colorado for All report due in December and the launch of a 2026 Inclusive Leadership cohort.

**[Colorado families hit from every angle as taxes and fees outpace income growth](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The latest analysis from the Common Sense Institute shows Denver-area households feeling a real financial squeeze, and it’s not just higher prices driving it. The report finds that since 2016, the typical household’s tax and fee load has jumped 48 percent while pre-tax income has grown only 27 percent.

**NATIONAL**
**[Trump announces full pardon of Tina Peters in Truth Social post](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) President Trump posted on Wednesday that he is granting a full pardon to former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters, who has served 14 months of a nine year prison sentence. He announced it in a Truth Social post late in the afternoon.
__
**[Kansas case and new immigration report deepen scrutiny of Colorado’s stance on SAVE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A Kansas mayor’s felony voting case is renewing national attention on how noncitizens end up registered to vote and what states can do to prevent it. The issue is gaining urgency as federal agencies expand the Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements program, known as SAVE, while Colorado and several other states reject federal data-sharing efforts even as federal law requires them to maintain accurate voter rolls.

**[Teen’s Suicide Highlights Rising Sextortion Crisis Targeting American Teens](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[NEW YORK POST](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) clean_body: The Tina Peters story took another turn this week — here’s the clear, complete update | | | | December 14, 2025 SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST | | Dear Rocky Mountain Voice family, It was a Trump voice HUGE week of news in Colorado. Such a big week that our website crashed a couple of times due to crazy amounts of traffic, thanks for that! We encourage you to read this edition in full and take time with our top articles below — this is the kind of news you don’t want to fall behind on. This week, no story came close to the reach or impact of the Tina Peters case. What began years ago as a local prosecution has now become one of the most closely watched election-integrity and civil-rights stories in the country — and our reporting helped push it into the national spotlight. How This Case Began Tina Peters is the former elected clerk of Mesa County, responsible for administering elections — including federal elections — under both state and federal law. In 2021, she authorized the preservation of election system records ahead of a scheduled software update. Peters has consistently maintained that she did so to comply with federal law , which requires election officials to preserve records tied to federal elections for 22 months. At trial, jurors were . Peters was convicted in state court and sentenced to nine years in prison. That unresolved conflict — between state prosecution and federal election law — is the foundation of this case. What Changed This Week Several major developments collided at once. Colorado quietly released — then heavily redacted — a letter from the U.S. Bureau of Prisons requesting that Peters be transferred to federal custody. An reviewed by Rocky Mountain Voice shows federal officials cited concerns about her confinement conditions and confirmed she has no federal charges. Nearly all of that explanation was removed before the letter was made public. On the same day a federal judge declined to intervene in Peters’ habeas petition — while acknowledging that her case raises “important constitutional questions” — she was placed into isolation confinement for 22 hours a day. That move came just as the U.S. Department of Justice announced a into conditions inside Colorado’s prison system, including the facility where Peters is held. Days later, President Trump announced a , granting Peters a full and unconditional pardon for offenses related to election integrity and security during the 2020–2021 period. This was not a symbolic gesture — it was a signed pardon issued under Article II of the Constitution and transmitted through the Department of Justice. Peters’ legal team immediately notified the Colorado Department of Corrections and arrived at the prison expecting her release. Colorado refused. Why the Pardon Is Being Disputed Colorado’s position is straightforward: presidential pardons apply to federal crimes , not state convictions. From the state’s perspective, Peters is a state inmate serving a state sentence, and the pardon has no effect. But Peters’ attorneys argue the case is not that simple. When local officials administer federal elections , they do so under federal authority , bound first by federal law. The U.S. Supreme Court has long held that election officials conducting federal elections operate under federal oversight. If Peters was prosecuted for actions taken to comply with federal election-record preservation laws — and that defense was never allowed before a jury — her attorneys argue the case falls within the President’s constitutional pardon power for “offenses against the United States.” That question has never been answered by a court. Why This Matters Beyond One Case This is no longer just about Tina Peters. It’s about whether states can punish election officials for complying with federal law — and then block federal review by labeling prosecutions “state-only." It’s about whether speech on election vulnerabilities can be used to justify harsher sentencing. And it’s about transparency, due process, and whether political narratives have replaced constitutional scrutiny. What Happens Next Peters remains in Colorado custody while her state appeal continues. Federal courts have acknowledged serious constitutional issues but have so far declined to intervene. Colorado has rejected both federal concern and a presidential pardon. The unresolved questions now move toward higher courts — and possibly the U.S. Supreme Court — where the clash between state authority and federal election law may finally be addressed. Until then, this case remains unfinished — and deeply consequential. And we will continue to follow it, report it, and explain it clearly — because Coloradans deserve to understand what’s happening in their own state, without filters, slogans, or selective silence. — Team RMV | | | | | TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK BY JEN SCHUMANN, The state confirmed it received a request from the Bureau of Prisons regarding the transfer of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters to federal custody. What the public has not been allowed to see is why the federal government made it in the first place. BY A.L. GOODWIN, On December 6, 2025, former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters formally submitted an application for a Presidential Pardon to President Donald J. Trump. Her legal team describes the request as both a matter of justice and national security, pointing to newly released evidence and expanding federal investigations that directly corroborate Peters’ original claims. BY SHAINA COLE, In early October, residents across Erie and in Virginia, began opening their mailboxes to find sympathy cards inside plain envelopes. The message referenced the assassination of Charlie Kirk, accompanied by an empty packet of Gushers candy taped to the card, glitter and a loose powdery substance that spilled when handled. | STATE After years of voter-approved measures scaling back Colorado’s income tax rate, proponents of significantly higher government spending have cleared a major hurdle at the state Title Board towards raising taxes by $4.1 billion annually. BY JEN SCHUMANN, When managers in the Colorado Department of Agriculture (CDA) logged into their Nov. 6 virtual meeting, the agenda read like a standard operational update. But on the list sat two items that created angst: the statewide EDI/Colorado for All report due in December and the launch of a 2026 Inclusive Leadership cohort. BY SHAINA COLE, The latest analysis from the Common Sense Institute shows Denver-area households feeling a real financial squeeze, and it’s not just higher prices driving it. The report finds that since 2016, the typical household’s tax and fee load has jumped 48 percent while pre-tax income has grown only 27 percent. | NATIONAL BY JEN SCHUMANN, President Trump posted on Wednesday that he is granting a full pardon to former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters, who has served 14 months of a nine year prison sentence. He announced it in a Truth Social post late in the afternoon. BY JEN SCHUMANN, A Kansas mayor’s felony voting case is renewing national attention on how noncitizens end up registered to vote and what states can do to prevent it. The issue is gaining urgency as federal agencies expand the Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements program, known as SAVE, while Colorado and several other states reject federal data-sharing efforts even as federal law requires them to maintain accurate voter rolls. clean_body: | | | Peter, This is something I’ve needed to say to you for a long time. There’s a reason I wanted to reach out to you before anyone else. I know that when I send you a message, it never falls on deaf ears. You’ve been a dedicated listener, Peter, and if this message is only read by one person like yourself, I’ll feel like I’ve done my duty. – Speaker Mike Johnson | | | | Paid for by Mike Johnson for Louisiana |

This ain't the same Party that it was when I first entered the big leagues. It's become a paradise for Liberal hogwash and downright anti-American behavior.
Whether it's...

* Rioting in cities across America and burning Old Glory
* Storming ICE facilities, doxxing agents, and throwing sandwiches at law enforcement
* Having actual COMMUNISTS run some of our greatest cities

It sure seems like once you think the Democrats can't stoop any lower, they grab a shovel and prove you wrong... Guess they never heard the first rule of holes: if you're in one, STOP DIGGING.
_But let me tell you the God-honest truth. They have the money to do it and if we don 't get our act together, they just might put us in their place._
Right now, the Democrats have a well-oiled fundraising machine that is raking in the big bucks from its out-of-touch, elitist donors - 100 million bucks, to be exact.
After seeing that, I knew I had to step in.
**_[So my good friend Majority Leader Steve Scalise and I got to talking. We think if we can get 10,000 God-fearing Conservatives to pitch in 10 bucks each, then we may just be able to dig our way out of this hole we're in.](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/hKLgsd3aUNzUopAfBTXKTJRhSQ99t6Hj24YQoUb9n4b5-bzPM0mUkTcD29I3hmtAkY72rXTx2b1MqqwJ2Q4dWoBIaL0qeAKhMN2u7m98IP7kKwamUtYYcMKj6izULpRpqfjmbm9Nx2OnP_Ql6nDN7WpZOnpyH2DpOpgr01_zn-yFeZA6QcFCucc85rwMIy0frah6NFSCK_cprCcds9kQBzCc6jzdjHmEo-UddBUO2uQh-Y_YZK63ADfhtVEI70GXtseEmPESqLL7kRH3x3memjrjDOTzF39x7-Yixe9GKLzV4oU2fL2lQvHskWIEdzlf9D1RP-0pgYFvpJJ0nlUFwYXIo47BL6YSjYSsS-_Jg1Uz1XO9kjs2l0fRN94p66zc7ViAIKsgzjlW5Wf4itFnzK2Ko3PWaKY2B0OH7YxMrOciQ_YxDF-Kzo9FwnYkUfP5dMKwCDs2scX_BYClanDdeBJx7ZhOxerrfiEIwfEDHBTomgwBA6CtUNS_knyFvFU27ZGjK7C-W4p5Lp8_-4bZD1MiPAPxJnBzEFYhV9jQlbHKsyWE425UuBxjdSjkGy8vUOiuNaPUAzHhfvsToJZ9ITyro2T0Sz324fNuVS4Oinn8SEYNycyuhWM63I2Xmib8ytDsrcTiPg3TjiSQOpmXkKmI70kky0EyMxO23hZPHy0oVMOwJda7LDNXr9lqxaCVhdd25f1uUA6tU0-j8xzWoYRTYhlLs4FwZHIQn6GKQtpSkvfdy1DztDxwffUq6Jvz3tWxzALqmhzzYFZmA_kGcl6VWaIGKBqO-Yj7JMXjp-w76KZ3DAZe-oqlBPyPk3WEB3lyGMs/1ZgCLGggCLiWyOAZfoLlPY7YEy4aBEHF/15)_**

clean_body: way these Democrats are acting up right now's got me madder than a wet hen in a tote sack. This ain't the same Party that it was when I first entered the big leagues. It's become a paradise for Liberal hogwash and downright anti-American behavior. Whether it's... Rioting in cities across America and burning Old Glory Storming ICE facilities, doxxing agents, and throwing sandwiches at law enforcement Having actual COMMUNISTS run some of our greatest cities It sure seems like once you think the Democrats can't stoop any lower, they grab a shovel and prove you wrong... Guess they never heard the first rule of holes: if you're in one, STOP DIGGING. But let me tell you the God-honest truth. They have the money to do it and if we don 't get our act together, they just might put us in their place. Right now, the Democrats have a well-oiled fundraising machine that is raking in the big bucks from its out-of-touch, elitist donors - 100 million bucks, to be exact. After seeing that, I knew I had to step in. Do you reckon you could be one of those Conservatives? we all step up and work together I think we'll be able to keep these Democrats at bay and continue to deliver wins for good, hard workin' Americans. But I can 't do it without you, Peter. Thanks for hearing me out, John N. Kennedy A personal note from Majority Leader Steve Scalise: I'm humbled to announce that I am your new House Majority Leader. My first order of business is checking in with our best supporters, so I just launched my new MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY for that specific reason. Christmas shopping is a headache. Finding gear that actually lasts? Even harder. That's why we're giving away **100 Tactical Fitness Bundles** — completely free — and they’ll arrive in **3–5 business days** , _before Christmas_. **What you get:** **Gift #1: Smart Fitness Watch** ✅ Tracks heart rate, steps, sleep, and performance ✅ Waterproof and built for impact ✅ Gives you the “intel” most men ignore until it’s too late **Gift #2: Hydration Backpack** ✅ Holds 2 liters of water — stays cold for hours ✅ Lightweight and field-tested tough ✅ Perfect for rucks, hikes, hunts, or long workouts No wrapping required. Now listen... You don’t have to be a hardcore workout guy to appreciate this. But chances are, you know **someone** who would. A son. A brother. A buddy. That one family member who actually trains. And this bundle? It’s the kind of gift that _actually gets used_. Whatever your mission is, we’ve got the supply. **If you claim this today, it ships in 3-5 business days.** It will be in your hands (or under the tree) well before Christmas. **Here’s the catch:** Only **100 bundles** are going out for free. # **[Ted Nugent's loud protest is the wake-up call Western elites want to ignore](http://trk.libertyledger.-PP0lQUw)**
---
Ted Nugent is known for many things. Subtlety isn't one of them.This is a man who treats volume knobs the way toddlers treat bedtime: with open defiance. So when a mosque in his Michigan town began broadcasting the early-morning call to prayer over loudspeakers, Nugent reacted in the way only Nugent would. He turned his back yard into a launchpad for a one-man rock assault.You don’t need to be religious to see the problem. You only need to have ears.Excessive? Perhaps.

Parler Technologies is hosting a live investor webinar and Q & A on Thursday, December 18, 2025at 9:30 AM PST / 12:30 PM EST. This session provides a detailed look at how Parler is building a fully owned digital ecosystem that connects cloud, CDN, payments, streaming, and commerce into one unified platform.

The leadership team will walk through how the company is scaling its infrastructure, expanding across multiple revenue channels, and continuing to grow its creator and merchant ecosystem. The presentation will also review the anticipated plan to pursue an IPO in 2026 and how the current Regulation D 506(c) round is structured for accredited investors.

You will hear how the company plans to:

- Strengthen and scale its infrastructure across cloud and edge systems
- Expand creator focused monetization and fintech capabilities
- Build long term value through a vertically integrated stack
- Position itself for its planned IPO

Attendance is open to accredited investors interested in Parler’s Regulation D 506(c) offering.

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=1f20b09907dbf4c2b444625a685819e49860b5c5806f4abb79bd626c70b89b3be881bc947eccc4c4ff26bcfcee942a1a5a13677beb7a906f52334f48655a8c6f
Register Now

If you cannot attend live, please register and we will send the replay to you once the session concludes or to schedule a time to speak with an investor relations representative, you can

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=1f20b09907dbf4c2ece537a02a4e79d8eb4de36875e376312c9fac8b1220aa39fb87c6039a832352e472d0f39b96add58b4109d1f08defb007ec12200550d47a
book a call here .

You can also reach us at

mailto:invest@parler.com
invest@parler.com
or call or text

tel:2145602565
(214) 560-2565
with any questions.

Best regards,

Parler Technologies Inc.

name: Defeat Lauren Boebert
subject: She’s cooked—these abysmal numbers spell doom
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
This is the surest sign yet that even her own donors see what we’ve known all along: she is cooked. The chaos, the scandals, the hate—it’s finally catching up to her.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Kiof6MdCFx3JDACEaNPuY2GPmM06ow3lzBTlyJ18u1ZJrBN1bqKv2DPrF-vxva_b0a7kBxRggFGVwA1Lr_1GLjbsXh5dpTyM8FR52ZxJG41M8eciWld7dud4lww7l9LpGYF2Z3hQrL34UoufY4OQaQgBcDa-ll64emTazbCEioSmWHG03fnuv3t3lPgtYcBx4wF00nhn_J3WbS5VT7WQVQ2GzjJrIiKD&c=rhX7DeXULm06hgEZOrl6LSK5gmTI3RESqn4BTpGxq4SBjHZy2RYPBQ==&ch=JpLkpwhEXUCbt3uQWDo9yffzHjq90lZtPMBEg49t5UWRPK2qDi9_fg==)
---
|

Peter,
Lauren Boebert's most recent fundraising report exposes just how vulnerable she really is.
She only raised $110,000.
That's not a war chest--that's a white flag.
It's the surest sign yet that even her own donors see what we've known all along: Boebert is cooked. The chaos, the scandals, the hate--it's finally catching up to her.
While Boebert's campaign burns to the ground, labor leader and fighter for working Coloradans Trisha Calvarese, the Democrat running to defeat Boebert, is surging.
Trisha just outraised Boebert 5 to 1. In 2024, when they went head to head, Trisha had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in the district and outperformed Kamala Harris by 6 points. Now it's time to finish the job.
Boebert is in Congress because of just 546 votes. She is the most vulnerable Republican in all of Congress. This is our moment to end this embarrassment once and for all.
[We can't take our foot off the gas. If we go all in, we can bury Boebert's 2026 reelection campaign before it even gets off the ground:](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Kiof6MdCFx3JDACEaNPuY2GPmM06ow3lzBTlyJ18u1ZJrBN1bqKv2DPrF-vxva_b0a7kBxRggFGVwA1Lr_1GLjbsXh5dpTyM8FR52ZxJG41M8eciWld7dud4lww7l9LpGYF2Z3hQrL34UoufY4OQaQgBcDa-ll64emTazbCEioSmWHG03fnuv3t3lPgtYcBx4wF00nhn_J3WbS5VT7WQVZ0cPSSQe4nl&c=rhX7DeXULm06hgEZOrl6LSK5gmTI3RESqn4BTpGxq4SBjHZy2RYPBQ==&ch=JpLkpwhEXUCbt3uQWDo9yffzHjq90lZtPMBEg49t5UWRPK2qDi9_fg==)
---

She only raised $110,000. That's not a war chest--that's a white flag. It's the surest sign yet that even her own donors see what we've known all along: Boebert is cooked. The chaos, the scandals, the hate--it's finally catching up to her. While Boebert's campaign burns to the ground, labor leader and fighter for working Coloradans Trisha Calvarese, the Democrat running to defeat Boebert, is surging. Trisha just outraised Boebert 5 to 1. In 2024, when they went head to head, Trisha had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in the district and outperformed Kamala Harris by 6 points. Now it's time to finish the job. Boebert is in Congress because of just 546 votes. She is the most vulnerable Republican in all of Congress. And I welcome her to this race," Cruz said during a Wednesday evening appearance on Fox News's "Hannity." subject: Bondi Beach Attack, 2026 Stamps, and a Pregnant Rockette
body:
Two gunmen opened fire on a [Hanukkah celebration](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1uwzAUg09jbw70nv6sQUOXbOna0VCkp1itbBmWmsC3L5wrdCNBgODHr9t0cymn9fHpFrItlmmlV83UGu3Tvawh3cn52bXm_A8yVLW5ZZuQoRwAB5BTcCkfk-iDjURIvCcLWklt1MhkP1uQhhuNLKAw4EkqySIpwX2QTozG98mebYAgAZgSeHEcOSoiHbkRJEQnGIXUyn75LmltM8V8lCftF1-WPtu5ta12_KPDa4dXt50AZ3SavSWf6VS_te0uJzfUuZSW1sdQj7DSMbwhhzflwCFqgMhMVFoHFseRGSBPKKOS4KHfbdheKedUle4Eeywu5feM9q_vnhb_AgAA__9P739h) at Bondi Beach in Sydney, Australia, on Sunday, killing at least 15 people, including a 10-year-old girl, and wounding 42 others in what authorities are treating as a terror attack.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbuq3DAQhp9G7rRIo4u9hYuA4-4EwgmkNFppbCvRxVham3374FwgTapMOcz3fwz_17fpzfjg0_LJROzrnKeEZwlYK-7TIyfnH2jsamo19jsw0KWauE3AQFEOlKvJGR9ek2xcPyMCigZ73mrV3nXHVLP2s2uN7rixRkFn1d0pySWDmbFOCTazxvdXGgeuOGdaws0IEKAR21ncJUpJJEPna95v37JPdcU5vPKB-83m2IR-rXUrRHwgMBIYz_O8bXuuaKtPS3wVc_i0FIrR-HARBMYlB0cPak1ZKQNoQV2sGG0iYuiUIqBDIWJgjNOPF0e_rEjHX1YCuszWOyKGlvHPK7tmAfv-TkA_a5ysiZvxS_rnQUTnn5GI4Wf272XJz90iEcNfqmbv3Xb6EHzRLZFs-fNDU_-rqqOHHwEAAP__NM6iCw) | |
# Monday, December 15, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1791, the [U.S. Bill of Rights](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DtuhDAQANDTmA5kj3_ZwkUauk2bEvkzLJMYvILRIm4fJVfIBV7xPu_TPVKl7fERVww8t2nD86jIjPuU2lYoYcxLZI75GyS4g-P6nECC7RX0yk4lUr0m05UwIwLqDoPyzvqbe5O2W4LCjKkkEz1kHRESeD3r2bok081n6Cj8agqUVUo6A0PUoMEh-lnfDBojjMRC3Pbhq9HGC871ai_ch9zWroaF-XkI_S5gFDCe5znUlodHewkYiXEVMHIr8epp6xc6uO2XgLFgxjXh3isrYOz2UJ4n1UqH88LIxxqp_vn8r5RXgJ8AAAD__yDBdAM) was ratified, becoming law and adding the first ten amendments to the Constitution. These amendments guaranteed fundamental freedoms, including freedom of speech, religion, the press, and due process. Today’s news includes a heartbreaking tragedy out of Australia, where a [mass shooting](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OozAURp_GdCDfa2ND4SLSii7bbolsuA7emB_hq6DM048yrzD1J3065_y7j3efctoef_1KjuM-bnSVTMx0jmHf5hTIT4tn9tMTJZrCfj1GlNjWgDW04-xTfo-6ml0kQlIVObCmtb3pZFstjqIGZYKlaYaeUFLsvEQKwZCdDMQquc8bILQA0mhsvEKFhshG1WvSWmhJc-L9bP7vaeOFYn7vLzqbaV-r7Bbmowh1EzgIHK7rakKYPpvA4SMjcMjpRQKH6fl4qh7eX6tloYZjLyzUH18KscBW3YJS0WtQdTQ91roDWXe9pdqAlAhtkKR0dbr5uFLOqRgrtHysPuUfEv5VvpfD7wAAAP__LL99wA) at Bondi Beach on the first night of Hanukkah left at least 15 people dead. We extend our deepest sympathies to the victims, their families, and the Jewish community as we report the details below. Tune into today’s _Flyover Podcast,_ where host Ayla Brown shares simple ways you can spread joy in your community through donations during a busy holiday season. Even the smallest act of kindness can go a long way in making someone’s holidays brighter. [Watch the episode here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzz1u4zAQQOHTUJ0FcvgnFywWG6hzmgBOKYzFocWEEg1pLMO3D5wrpHvVB7zP03DCXPJyfceZAqc6LPTYCjHTOlzqEvOFcJyQGcdvkOA2xvk2gAR7UHBQdoiYy3MwTQyJCEg3FJR31h9dJ20zhejGkZyTCVMig1pZr7xF1MrLTh91k8NLU6CsUtIZaFGDBkfkkz4aMkYYSTFzXduvmheeKJVn3Wltxzo3JUzMt03ofwJ6Af2z3vneXuiV_f_9cd4_zt0mdM9Cv-lONmuIt0cuJW_OCyOvM-byK_Gf9vcAPwEAAP__iyxq4w). If you’ve ever wondered whether your savings are truly keeping up with the times, this eye-opening comparison from today's sponsor, [GoldCo](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbuq3DAQhp9G7rRIo4u9hYuA4-4EwgmkNFppbCvRxVham3374FwgTapMOcz3fwz_17fpzfjg0_LJROzrnKeEZwlYK-7TIyfnH2jsamo19jsw0KWauE3AQFEOlKvJGR9ek2xcPyMCigZ73mrV3nXHVLP2s2uN7rixRkFn1d0pySWDmbFOCTazxvdXGgeuOGdaws0IEKAR21ncJUpJJEPna95v37JPdcU5vPKB-83m2IR-rXUrRHwgMBIYz_O8bXuuaKtPS3wVc_i0FIrR-HARBMYlB0cPak1ZKQNoQV2sGG0iYuiUIqBDIWJgjNOPF0e_rEjHX1YCuszWOyKGlvHPK7tmAfv-TkA_a5ysiZvxS_rnQUTnn5GI4Wf272XJz90iEcNfqmbv3Xb6EHzRLZFs-fNDU_-rqqOHHwEAAP__NM6iCw), between cash and gold might change the way you think about protecting your wealth.

## 15 Killed in Bondi Beach Hanukkah Shooting
Two gunmen opened fire on a [Hanukkah celebration](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1uwzAUg09jbw70nv6sQUOXbOna0VCkp1itbBmWmsC3L5wrdCNBgODHr9t0cymn9fHpFrItlmmlV83UGu3Tvawh3cn52bXm_A8yVLW5ZZuQoRwAB5BTcCkfk-iDjURIvCcLWklt1MhkP1uQhhuNLKAw4EkqySIpwX2QTozG98mebYAgAZgSeHEcOSoiHbkRJEQnGIXUyn75LmltM8V8lCftF1-WPtu5ta12_KPDa4dXt50AZ3SavSWf6VS_te0uJzfUuZSW1sdQj7DSMbwhhzflwCFqgMhMVFoHFseRGSBPKKOS4KHfbdheKedUle4Eeywu5feM9q_vnhb_AgAA__9P739h) at Bondi Beach in Sydney, Australia, on Sunday, killing at least 15 people, including a 10-year-old girl, and wounding 42 others in what authorities are treating as a terror attack. The suspects, believed to be father and son aged 50 and 24, began firing on the crowd with rifles from a footbridge during the _Hanukkah by the Sea_ event. Together, men [legally owned six firearms](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpTAMRb8mdH4CE8K8IsU2dLPtlsiPOJDdkKDEM-j9_Qp-YRo3ls695_75nD8pxJDW37SzFZ_nxGeNLMJlfuXkwotp2UiEln_YoqlC-zFjiwN0CN0wOwrxPevGWc-M3Ddsu9EM49N8tEOz2dYtBhfjiBbfjjx6HvUThxFJ69cH-SbYi9ZhN3RdazQ-qMceDfPo-6dmrZVu2QXJ5fE3hyQb-_jO31weS96baDeRo6r-l8JJ4ZTeR65yvRROF1jh1OF1tMLpzCU6uBQVTrce3H4gXEouoUqFkwuDJ9m4ACUHNSc4twyRV4rxDflM7MDA-pUqOK5HEAbJX-sGPhSmsleIdCc0xbrjDDGGakal23WnEO_a8qOtvy3-DwAA__93_pLS) despite Australia's strict gun laws. Beachgoers fled the scene as the attack continued for 20 minutes, with the gunmen reloading and firing multiple times. The massacre was disrupted by Muslim fruit stand owner Ahmed al Ahmed, 43, who snuck up behind, tackled, and disarmed one gunman, despite suffering two gunshot wounds in the process. Watch [the video of the moment here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0Dtu6zAQheHVUJ0FcviyCxYXuHDnBEmTUuBjZDGhREMai3BWHzhbSHsO8BX_x2W4-Fzycn3xMzoa67Bg2woS4TqEuqQc0MfJE_n4BRzMRn6-DcBBHwQchB6Sz-UxqC65ERFQduiENdqezJHrbnI2RtAxJB6kDsIko0fEox2lNSLwU-qye2oChBaCGwW9lyDBINpRnhQqxRTHlKmu_WfNC004lkfdce1jnbviJqLbxuQ_BmcG59Za_6h3ugd8_s_FU5yYPO9M_r8cm513eH_9futWl24tl5I3Y5ni19nn8kvSnzrsDn4CAAD__w21bz0). The elder gunman was shot and killed by police, who later searched the suspects’ vehicle and home, discovering homemade explosives. The other gunman was hospitalized in critical condition. [Follow live updates here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OozAURp_GdCDfa2ND4SLSii7bbolsuA7emB_hq6DM048yrzD1J3065_y7j3efctoef_1KjuM-bnSVTMx0jmHf5hTIT4tn9tMTJZrCfj1GlNjWgDW04-xTfo-6ml0kQlIVObCmtb3pZFstjqIGZYKlaYaeUFLsvEQKwZCdDMQquc8bILQA0mhsvEKFhshG1WvSWmhJc-L9bP7vaeOFYn7vLzqbaV-r7Bbmowh1EzgIHK7rakKYPpvA4SMjcMjpRQKH6fl4qh7eX6tloYZjLyzUH18KscBW3YJS0WtQdTQ91roDWXe9pdqAlAhtkKR0dbr5uFLOqRgrtHysPuUfEv5VvpfD7wAAAP__LL99wA).
## Brown University Shooting Suspect Arrested
Police detained a [person of interest](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u3CAQRp8Gd6xg8G9BESlyd9NEUUoLw7CehAUL5u7Kbx9tXuHWn_TpnPP7Y_twlCjff7gHWo5ly_hqCZmxbnvJgXZ0_nDMzv8FBWNj9zg3UDBIDVIPW3CUrq3vgo2IgKZDq6dxmJZxVkN32KA17Mor0w9xNovSizJx8H6BfZ-Mnjuy7zcNetBajT3cnAEDI-IUzdJj34teYSAu9fanUOYDY7rKE-vNl0eX7MF8NmG-CVgFrG73b4PbvbxnAeuvnwLWE2srWVJmrNhY-s_GJVzSRcYq91peWX5memJtxJdsRylM-S5LjOTJJQFr41IvYVYKwnzXMJt5XsB01YbzRSlRGyfRq_vDUfoPxl-q-bTwLwAA__-TwIed) early Sunday at a Coventry, Rhode Island, hotel, approximately 28 miles from where Saturday's deadly Brown University shooting left two dead and nine injured during an economics final exam review. The man in his mid-20s from Wisconsin, not a Brown student, was allegedly carrying two firearms when apprehended around 3:45 a.m., including a weapon with unique characteristics that helped tie him to the attack. The man allegedly yelled something before firing [more than 40 rounds](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSrCAQRb8GM6agRRkDgpeYzUs3tEAbZRfBgp6Z9e-33F_Y5CTddere-_GYHjbEkNb_dkdDPk8J3zUiEZbJ5bQEh3beLJGdv0BAX8nuxwQCOi6By25abIjnpJrFeETAtkEjdd_pob-LrtmMtx0IoXFG3UmtB-mlVxqHu4O5db1rgrlsEmQnpegV3GwLLfSI2reDQqWYErgEyuX2mUOiDX088wvLbc57E81GdFTW_mMwMhjTeeRK14nBeIkZjBIuKAbjs_KrH4PRlfxO_JnCC0sNdPKjZIcX14K1hrRy_KaCO8aTe1uJW09Y-JyPyt2zEj-w1Jx49jwkwoLXC_EtE0YGY1PMcrxDjKH2mimx7jbE38T0p5lfBn4CAAD__3hfkWM) from a 9mm handgun, though witnesses couldn't make out what was screamed. Brown has [canceled all remaining classes](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHSnCAUBeCn0Q4HroBSUKTZ7k-b0rnCZSU_giPsmn37jHmF9N-cOef8-lq-MKaYnz9xJ9tCWTJdNVFrdC5ryT6uhG7D1tB9AwddG-7HAhwUE8CEWjzG9Flk720gAhp7smLSajJ65qrfbHCcjzpMUrtVO2VmMsa7OczkjUIc-2jvNAFCCcG1hAFHGEETTWE0kqTsJCcfWzmH3yXmtlFIn_Kmc3Bl75PdWjtqN_7o4NHB47qu4Z5wEX3foIPHepYrs1eObzprbB_mMDtKlbmEtVJl9Af3yjA0Opkn9Okm-_GqrG6ltJifTAjgxijZn9YfV0wpVj11kj93jOlfkfZf770t_A0AAP__DBCEUw), exams, and projects for the fall semester, with officials stating that the investigation is "progressing extremely fast," although no charges have been filed yet.
## USPS Reveals 2026 Stamps
The U.S. Postal Service (USPS) has unveiled its [2026 stamp lineup](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OwyAUBODT2B0WPP7igmKbdNl2S4uYR8wGg2WIUW6_cq6w_WhG3_zcppsNMaTHt13RVJ-nhK1ErBX36Z6TC3e082JrtfMTKKhS7bpNQEESBoTJydkQ35PonfGIgLxHw7SSelQXKvvFeK5HwecLzn50fqTSUw2g7SioV4Je-mDONgZMMkaVgMFy4KAQteejQCE6QdGFmvfhN4dUF_TxnQ_chzmvfTRLrVvp-FcH1w6urbXhJDTE5xno4PoqWyGvdGCIhSRs5GMoxOednCTCGFAlNfS7cVsLMYaidCfoY7Uhflbqv645DPwFAAD___iodNw) as the agency prepares to mark its 250th anniversary in 2026. The new stamps honor icons like Muhammad Ali, Bruce Lee, and Harriet Powers. Other designs highlight American cultural symbols such as lowriders. You can see images of the [stamps here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOqzAQRb_GdEb2YAMuXLyGLq_dEg14CN4YjPBsovz9ivzCdiONdO499-s23jCmuN__40aelzzu9CqJmOkcp7yHOBHOKzLj_AAFbWHcjhEUWKlBajsGjOk9mir4hQioqcjrrrWda3tlq9XP2PeqcxbQdqqdgpusdkCmp-DCorGK_qJp0FZr1RqosYEGWqJuaZwhY4RRFCLns_7OceeVlvTOTzrrOW9V8ivzUUTzT8AgYLjq1z_lKNdXwHCxBQxaXbcTMKAsO-FDHkQPiSx5JfmRKjIv8lIUMFSnD8crphRL2wmj7hvG9MnjP4309PAbAAD__3n3diw). Additional designs celebrate American space exploration with images captured by NASA’s James Webb telescope. They can be purchased online, by phone, by mail, or in select post offices. Officially licensed stamp products are [also available on Amazon](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2uozAYAE9jOhD-_F-4SDa4y7ZbIsf-CN41GIEVlD39U94VXj3SjDR_7uPdp5zW52-_oK1TGVc8j4y14j4-yhrTA32Yfa0-_IMe5FH9so3Qg2gptFSM0af8HnkT7YQIyBq0VEmhjNS9aGZrgGoudQwh8D4ogVI8dDBBRgUamGmS_dgoUEFpLzl0ngEDiagmZjhyTniPMdWyd39LWuuMU36XF-5dKEuT7VzrdhB2IeAIuPM8O7_4_2X9YALuqGXHg4Db_BMJOG7ADQMb2tuVQcsH7VpzEa69iV96MAMofr01u43bmXJOh1SE98_Fp_ydqz969LLwFQAA__9n4XRS). Here are the [release dates](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAQhZ8Gd1gwgJ0UFLdxl9tuaWE8BHbxj2A2Ud5-RaR9gm2mmJG-Od_5uM03l3La7__dhpbCMe_4rBmJsMzLsa9pQeejI3L-CwQMldx2ziDAcAlcmnl1Kb9m3a02IAKqDq0cBzNeh4swXbQrhCUofzXCC6MuYnHajSr4xSsfghRdso0mQRopxaChdwoUDIhjUFeNWjMtcE10lP7zSDtFDPl1PLD0_ti6bCPRWZn6x2BiMLX4_Xc9a7symBqbwSThd0QsWPkz4s4pIg-pVOLNjL_VKn-mnLkrJT2QwdQVu55tleowMi3um0v5_Zn-VNfDwk8AAAD__zt7ezU) for the first batch of 2026 stamps, along with the cities hosting their dedication ceremonies.

clean_body: Two gunmen opened fire on a at Bondi Beach in Sydney, Australia, on Sunday, killing at least 15 people, including a 10-year-old girl, and wounding 42 others in what authorities are treating as a terror attack. | | | | | | Monday, December 15, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1791, the was ratified, becoming law and adding the first ten amendments to the Constitution. These amendments guaranteed fundamental freedoms, including freedom of speech, religion, the press, and due process. Today’s news includes a heartbreaking tragedy out of Australia, where a at Bondi Beach on the first night of Hanukkah left at least 15 people dead. We extend our deepest sympathies to the victims, their families, and the Jewish community as we report the details below. Tune into today’s Flyover Podcast, where host Ayla Brown shares simple ways you can spread joy in your community through donations during a busy holiday season. Even the smallest act of kindness can go a long way in making someone’s holidays brighter. . If you’ve ever wondered whether your savings are truly keeping up with the times, this eye-opening comparison from today's sponsor, , between cash and gold might change the way you think about protecting your wealth. | 15 Killed in Bondi Beach Hanukkah Shooting Two gunmen opened fire on a at Bondi Beach in Sydney, Australia, on Sunday, killing at least 15 people, including a 10-year-old girl, and wounding 42 others in what authorities are treating as a terror attack. The suspects, believed to be father and son aged 50 and 24, began firing on the crowd with rifles from a footbridge during the Hanukkah by the Sea event. Together, men despite Australia's strict gun laws. Beachgoers fled the scene as the attack continued for 20 minutes, with the gunmen reloading and firing multiple times. The massacre was disrupted by Muslim fruit stand owner Ahmed al Ahmed, 43, who snuck up behind, tackled, and disarmed one gunman, despite suffering two gunshot wounds in the process. Watch . The elder gunman was shot and killed by police, who later searched the suspects’ vehicle and home, discovering homemade explosives. The other gunman was hospitalized in critical condition. . Brown University Shooting Suspect Arrested Police detained a early Sunday at a Coventry, Rhode Island, hotel, approximately 28 miles from where Saturday's deadly Brown University shooting left two dead and nine injured during an economics final exam review. The man in his mid-20s from Wisconsin, not a Brown student, was allegedly carrying two firearms when apprehended around 3:45 a.m., including a weapon with unique characteristics that helped tie him to the attack. The man allegedly yelled something before firing from a 9mm handgun, though witnesses couldn't make out what was screamed. Brown has , exams, and projects for the fall semester, with officials stating that the investigation is "progressing extremely fast," although no charges have been filed yet. USPS Reveals 2026 Stamps The U.S. Postal Service (USPS) has unveiled its as the agency prepares to mark its 250th anniversary in 2026. The new stamps honor icons like Muhammad Ali, Bruce Lee, and Harriet Powers. Other designs highlight American cultural symbols such as lowriders. You can see images of the .
