|
Peter,
When I first ran for office in 2018, I was a young candidate running to be a Cook County Commissioner--trying to break into a political system that wasn't built for people like me.
I was offered a contribution of $56,500 from a corporate PAC.
For a first-time candidate running for a county-level seat, that is a staggering amount of money. That is the type of check that can swing an election. It's the kind of money that propels careers and secures victories.
But I didn't even have to think about it.
I refused the check.
---
| | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001n6D1taJg4HMCwNLZ9GFYX03Qm8IU-1DmZfFCZYEZ3m-Jo36l_qdnBQnznDbTiAgnbE-A_lSB6s8nxAluQvNwE2usIudvEHlXEW-2bNIu-zQnZu7Q4NDFIvAb_bdMqsHR4yfNoU5rez9vONe6kiPn3tKRihfpIyUnmkfX4t-Cd3YfRM1G5claqYZa__MigfyTDj2ezcaEoa_9ziVK2UZdnJ-YtdRFCLuH&c=UZe4cY4q76wm5GppcOT4JWIDt4V69Nr5D-UvQt-5u9DqzMKSPuM7-A==&ch=cE38sbxGl8BUtHEJO5YppnB997L7wBtEpodFaWgzNw_u4zHkZNgimQ==)
---
| Now, in my campaign to be the next Congresswoman for IL-9, these same special interests and corporations are back--circling this open seat like vultures, looking for any candidate willing to take their money and do their bidding in Congress.
I made a choice back then to rely on people, not corporate PACs. It's a harder road, but it's the only one worth taking.
Groups like AIPAC are geared to spend up to seven figures to try and buy this seat. It's going to take everything we have to counter their spending, and because I won't take their money, I rely on grassroots supporters like you to fuel this movement.
[So I am asking you today: If you believe we need to get big money out of politics and elect leaders who can't be bought, will you chip in $25, $10 or whatever you can afford to help send this movement from the streets of Chicago to the halls of Congress?](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001n6D1taJg4HMCwNLZ9GFYX03Qm8IU-1DmZfFCZYEZ3m-Jo36l_qdnBQnznDbTiAgnDUYBSu1zzgIyW_yzHnfImdiN3PVP1Gq3VQdkLqyYHR9ioqppQQTf6hE8b-gFAVIC-a4nx8UnL-VrroEyLyvyctdpXBH7-UI87iba5qIecA2sPlD55MwRln5rDUMsNlnhXpnI-IUIBlQClu2vpQqQ-Sh79_ptJcGOz0GHJzHp5hw=&c=UZe4cY4q76wm5GppcOT4JWIDt4V69Nr5D-UvQt-5u9DqzMKSPuM7-A==&ch=cE38sbxGl8BUtHEJO5YppnB997L7wBtEpodFaWgzNw_u4zHkZNgimQ==)

Friend, Tennessee’s special election should’ve been a slam dunk. Instead, Republicans _barely_ held the seat, with just a 9-point win in a district President Trump carried by nearly 20. **That’s a wakeup call. And if we don’t listen, we’ll lose.** I went on Fox News and laid out, step-by-step, what we need to do to win in 2026.

Here’s the issue we face: Some of the voters who show up for President Trump don’t show up when he’s not on the ballot. But here’s the good news: **It doesn’t have to be that way.** We overcame it in Florida in 2022. This state was a purple battleground. Remember, we were winning by one point just a few years ago. But in 2022, we won by 1.5 million votes...a **20-point margin**. It was the biggest Republican victory in the state’s history. **How?** We didn’t run on pale pastels. We ran on **bold colors**. We drew sharp contrasts. clean_body: This is a wakeup call| | | Friend, Tennessee’s special election should’ve been a slam dunk. Instead, Republicans barely held the seat, with just a 9-point win in a district President Trump carried by nearly 20. That’s a wakeup call. And if we don’t listen, we’ll lose. I went on Fox News and laid out, step-by-step, what we need to do to win in 2026. Here’s the issue we face: Some of the voters who show up for President Trump don’t show up when he’s not on the ballot. But here’s the good news: It doesn’t have to be that way. We overcame it in Florida in 2022. This state was a purple battleground. Remember, we were winning by one point just a few years ago. But in 2022, we won by 1.5 million votes...a 20-point margin. It was the biggest Republican victory in the state’s history. How? We didn’t run on pale pastels. We ran on bold colors. We drew sharp contrasts. We stuck it to the Left every single day, and we delivered real results. If Republicans want to win, we need to give voters something to believe in, not just something to vote against. We’re electing bold conservatives across the country who don’t flinch, don’t fold, and don’t back down. Leaders who will stand up for parents' rights, border security, economic sanity, and American strength, just like we’ve done in Florida. Help us take the Florida Blueprint nationwide! This moment demands action. Not next year. Not next summer. Right now. 2026 is right around the corner , and if we’re going to build a winning majority, we’ve got to get in the fight today. Don’t wait. Help us recruit, support, and elect battle-ready conservatives across the country. Onward, Onward, Gov. Ron DeSantis | | | | | | Paid for by Restore Our Nation (RON PAC). Not Authorized by any Candidate or Candidate’s Committee. www.ronpac.com | PO Box 3696 Tallahassee, FL 32315 |

clean_body: Peter, I'm not alone in believing you truly deserve this. My colleagues and I at House GOP Headquarters were all in agreement. In fact, we've made a unanimous decision. If there's anyone who has a record worthy of this, it's you. First of all, Congratulations! This is reserved only for top supporters of our movement. But I have to say, you've been so outstanding in your record of support that you've built up a profile that falls within the TOP Tier of GOP Patriots. one of House GOP's most trusted patriots, and you deserve to have the status to reflect that! And before you click, I want to let you know that we'll be leaning on you for your opinion on matters as we move forward. I'm putting this deal on the table because we can't afford to lose our most trusted allies when our majority is as thin as it is right now! You, God Bless You and God Bless America! subject: Alaska’s Strength, Nationwide Impact: Strengthening Democracy Together
body:
You often hear from us regarding our political work. We are preparing for this next cycle, and thank you in advance for anything you can do to help us elect reasonable candidates on the federal side. But the reason I’m reaching out today is to tell you more about some of the education work that our sister organization is doing with Build Alaska’s Future Education Fund.| | |
---
| | Dear Supporter, You often hear from us regarding our political work. We are preparing for the upcoming election cycle and thank you in advance for anything you can do to [help us elect reasonable candidates on the federal side.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121341008/589131992/1172432375?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MGQ3NDdiMC1mNmQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3tfQEu3Kz5Kkhydm0zBPfxgiK7evNvCtPONWQvJGdUg=&emci=14ea4d67-75d7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=50d747b0-f6d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=9512399#contribute) However, the reason I’m reaching out today is to tell you about the education work of our sister organization, the 501c3 nonprofit [Build Alaska’s Future Education Fund (BAFEF)](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121341009/589131993/-1512324198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MGQ3NDdiMC1mNmQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3tfQEu3Kz5Kkhydm0zBPfxgiK7evNvCtPONWQvJGdUg=&emci=14ea4d67-75d7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=50d747b0-f6d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=9512399). In Alaska, strong local government and informed civic participation strengthen our communities, and when Alaska is strong, the rest of the nation grows stronger as well. **ClerkStart remains a centerpiece of BAFEF's education and storytelling efforts, producing five-minute video spotlights that illuminate the vital roles clerks and local government professionals play in safeguarding democracy across more than 230 communities.** These stories advance nonpartisan civic education, workforce development, and outreach designed to strengthen governance and public trust.

body:
Elon Musk just received one of the biggest compensation deals in U.S. history — nearly $1 trillion.| | | [](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=192914311849450e8c4d7d18dbf83db9&_e=JBqLM6wH8zNHBLlDf2hAutzkJpkycbPwKjOuYGiK0KtkLBLJyan4B5QKQmTcOVc2mVcKJ6-dDj91ihqa6jVuiN2udt1XKW9g3mZWr7mHhNmKnJOubHhOC-J2Q-m1dXCUCeNxhxMREYme2ZbAH6RhG7UYixBojeaOI24gpc9bcZqSzu8_XjsLXQy56-nTC_7QdtUl4Rfp2upVbq7J4544WZTUlC8KrjLhq3dSemaMsduc2E5II70uB6hdG9DkBSMB4jCWXKB-a84XkJ-bIDpPxoRh8SABdkO15ddvhedcOrpDP571NyottgJn1afdpQYRY3RBPygmHjRPV32Oi2pxzg%3D%3D)

| | **Musk Gets $1 Trillion — Trump Strikes Back**
---
| [ Watch the Presentation ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=192914311849450e8c4d7d18dbf83db9&_e=JBqLM6wH8zNHBLlDf2hAuldPCfLF_U4jDp_lhkoB6wyxwsg2O-Lg-jXVSdCQ3eOXHWtS6N2V4Vrsvoxh_tzKXpsZ9-DRSRwGpTBc_m5kpWyhLBTGXrRVxIaZCixDknirRa-0EMMupY8ZUCwxbjqhVA_kfjSrAoy0Dhrhhl9crchH33MaR3Npn1uQwMv1nLNqEBl5wd5vPQXG0OX3btQUgh6POy0AT9eKHvqLoqS2bCU0BhCnHqVO08IwUxcJZq5LpfQXiWR5uDG0dqrZbs01geYLn_DcrqXMbepM5QxxrSMP6LRzhl2t4-lEX3fH37pj83DbMudcNXd2RdUr0M3--SUPeNiDlNZAvMo8KQI9LuUBkqgtqZLP-z6IUpgz9YogZW5vcBm00AUqSRtAne0JcTWnN2wuXMeObR69AKIIlXMDSHS2x0Fy7ogMq_GGwslLJs6Cs7Fmt-HO2JLnIKqQFHj_Ys8ydMHtgruxqExLUfMGhxIlYRzntBsBJ65zqYwjs2TJlTdaMk2tygRgbgjXa4S-qf08cVY4yQeaWbpv2oC2KvZMGwsKGt0_4g1jlATpGLeuFrNCHXWqFS69SdE8Fw%3D%3D)

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Elon Musk just received one of the biggest compensation deals in U.S. history — **nearly $1 trillion.**

And while the headlines focus on him... Washington is already moving.
| [ ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=192914311849450e8c4d7d18dbf83db9&_e=JBqLM6wH8zNHBLlDf2hAuldPCfLF_U4jDp_lhkoB6wyxwsg2O-Lg-jXVSdCQ3eOXHWtS6N2V4Vrsvoxh_tzKXpsZ9-DRSRwGpTBc_m5kpWyhLBTGXrRVxIaZCixDknirRa-0EMMupY8ZUCwxbjqhVA_kfjSrAoy0Dhrhhl9crchH33MaR3Npn1uQwMv1nLNq5Xsm3Ys7cx7QEW3mEBEGdGgcr-Yj2hU1wIyLR3K43VamFGcYRp5BNmR3YW5P3DAPFuHdFCTY9t5kc3WLHVkKbAhajhFITjXtzfg0Jd4TqJvYur7Owec8XkzqVIR5HXNUMAlzv01zcoHccm31Io7JzTo7aDnlx6sDIQoDdzC5DhKGVugwucCPAqx7euRIz_kOb64cewamVT98oLE48r9_RNorLRQn9OH6KXNu3DiXQiU-OJvDLb7yV5OjbCxdoo80I-gW1WyF2tpiZWXSy6Zaw44ZrrUuhqDhZlLzbVy6jK2_-svkq5yrm61l3M0qXX8EacNEhjzwZZZkpOc3dAjYTT746--CNi066wOTrA10jAoplvSS1-K_t3sS11Y8Qbh2-YFMubFezDHn6Kd6psx8rw%3D%3D)
---
Because behind this record-setting payout, something larger is forming — a shift in power, policy and money flow.

One man is ready to leverage it.

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.
---

clean_body: Elon Musk just received one of the biggest compensation deals in U.S. history — nearly $1 trillion.| | | | December 15 | | | | | Musk Gets $1 Trillion — Trump Strikes Back | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Friend, Tennessee’s special election should’ve been a slam dunk. Instead, Republicans _barely_ held the seat, with just a 9-point win in a district President Trump carried by nearly 20. **That’s a wakeup call. And if we don’t listen, we’ll lose.** I went on Fox News and laid out, step-by-step, what we need to do to win in 2026.

clean_body: Here’s the step-by-step strategy we need to follow in 2026. | | | Friend, Tennessee’s special election should’ve been a slam dunk. Instead, Republicans barely held the seat, with just a 9-point win in a district President Trump carried by nearly 20. That’s a wakeup call. And if we don’t listen, we’ll lose. I went on Fox News and laid out, step-by-step, what we need to do to win in 2026. Here’s the issue we face: Some of the voters who show up for President Trump don’t show up when he’s not on the ballot. But here’s the good news: It doesn’t have to be that way. We overcame it in Florida in 2022. This state was a purple battleground. Remember, we were winning by one point just a few years ago. But in 2022, we won by 1.5 million votes...a 20-point margin. It was the biggest Republican victory in the state’s history. How? We didn’t run on pale pastels. We ran on bold colors. We drew sharp contrasts. We stuck it to the Left every single day, and we delivered real results. If Republicans want to win, we need to give voters something to believe in, not just something to vote against. We’re electing bold conservatives across the country who don’t flinch, don’t fold, and don’t back down. Leaders who will stand up for parents' rights, border security, economic sanity, and American strength, just like we’ve done in Florida. Help us take the Florida Blueprint nationwide! This moment demands action. Not next year. Not next summer. Right now. 2026 is right around the corner , and if we’re going to build a winning majority, we’ve got to get in the fight today. Don’t wait. Help us recruit, support, and elect battle-ready conservatives across the country. Onward, Gov. Ron DeSantis | | | | | | Paid for by Restore Our Nation (RON PAC). Team, at this point, there’s nothing President Trump could say that would shock me. His tirades and rants make the news every day.

_And last week, he directed one at me._

Trump attacked me personally because he doesn’t like that I stand up to the far-right’s chaos and corruption. Even more than that, Trump is terrified that this campaign can defeat his handpicked candidate here in NC and take back power for the American people.

Look, I’m not afraid of the president calling me names or riling up his MAGA base against me. But I know that this is just the start of the GOP directing all their attention at us. We’ve seen it time and time again: When Donald Trump attacks a candidate, the GOP money-machine revs up against them, too.

[**I need your help to face what’s ahead. I’m asking you to split a donation between our campaign and Lisa Blunt Rochester of whatever you can afford so we can have the resources to fight back.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342196/589135394/-1126108870?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.15_Cooper-Dec_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNmZlY2QwNy1mOWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NrDMRCCb5ZaHyRliaFNFsRoLZrfTruuPYNS5vfDW3Ys=)

So, you may be wondering what exactly Trump said about me.

He called me a _“radical left lunatic.”_

**If fighting for lower prices, affordable health care, abortion access, voting rights, good schools, and a livable planet makes me a “radical left lunatic,” then so be it.**

You know where I stand and what I’m fighting for, team. I need to know if you’re with me.

[**I’m not asking because I want to, but because I have to. This is the most consequential Senate race on the map. Even more than that, Trump is terrified that this campaign can defeat his handpicked candidate here in NC and take back power for the American people. Look, I’m not afraid of the president calling me names or riling up his MAGA base against me. But I know that this is just the start of the GOP directing all their attention at us. name: Urgent AI Update via Our Revolution
subject: Add your name: Stop the Big Tech oligarchs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

Donald Trump just signed a sweeping Executive Order on artificial
intelligence - and, as usual, it's a massive handout to his billionaire
cronies desperate to maximize their profits, no matter the disastrous
consequences.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34866?source=email-20251215&t=1011&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Headline: Trump signs executive order blocking states from enforcing
their own regulations around AI

This is Trump's latest attempt to give Big Tech free rein to develop AI
with no guardrails. And now he and his tech CEO buddies are pressing
Congress to go even further by passing a law that would rip away the
rights of states to protect us from AI abuses by undermining state-level
AI regulations.

We MUST stop them. But to do it it's going to take all of us speaking out
to raise the pressure.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34866?source=email-20251215&t=1012&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Together, let’s demand Congress put people before profit and put
democracy before Big Tech. Click here to add your name to stop Trump’s Big
Tech AI power grab.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34866?source=email-20251215&t=1013&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]ADD YOUR NAME

Trump's move isn't just dangerous — it's completely reckless.

The AI preemption bill Trump is pushing would give Big Tech a free pass to
develop powerful, risky AI with virtually NO oversight, wiping out
state-level safeguards on child safety, civil liberties, public health,
discrimination, harassment, financial scams, and more.

Trump may want to bulldoze any rules that could limit the profits of his
Big Tech cronies — but what we ACTUALLY need is stronger, smarter
protections to make sure AI is safe for everyone.

Let's be clear: This isn't about innovation or "efficiency." This push to
preempt state laws is about letting CEOs hide in the shadows, amass more
data, wield more power, and leave our communities vulnerable — all while
state leaders are left helpless to act.

At Our Revolution, we aren't waiting for the federal government to act to
rein in AI. Our organizers on the ground are already fighting to pass
meaningful AI protections state by state across the country — and we will
not let Trump and his tech oligarchs handcuff that work just to boost
their bottom line.

That's why it's so important we stop Congress from slipping Trump's latest
gift to tech billionaires into a must-pass bill. State leaders are
speaking up. Civil rights groups are mobilizing. But Congress needs to
hear from the grassroots — before it's too late.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34866?source=email-20251215&t=1014&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Will you add your name to our urgent petition and demand Congress
reject Trump's AI industry giveaway?

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34866?source=email-20251215&t=1015&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]ADD YOUR NAME

We've seen what happens when Big Tech is let off the leash. From
discriminatory algorithms to AI surveillance targeting protesters, it's
communities that always pay the price — not the CEOs. This is the moment
to draw a line and demand oversight that actually protects people, not
profits.

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Facebook | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10?t=1002&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Twitter | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/11?t=1003&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Instagram | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2355?t=1004&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Youtube | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2356?t=1005&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Website |
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10247?t=1006&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Sign Up

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26879?c0=email-20251215.kl3uGI&t=1007&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Our membership swag

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive
group in America.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26880?c0=email-20251215._2Liia&t=1008&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the
establishment by committing to be a Movement Builder with a recurring
donation of just $5 or more a month!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26881?c0=email-20251215.GaBB9W&t=1009&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]GIVE MONTHLY

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26882?c0=email-20251215.eKCw_D&t=1010&akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8 ]GIVE ONCE

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.ourrevolution.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-2/?akid=9974%2E5885482%2EjlzME8

Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe

PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
PO Box 66208
WASHINGTON, DC, 20035

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a
501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as
charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are
made to support Our Revolution’s general mission and are not designated for
any specific activity.

name: Team MGP Updates
subject: Support rural kids, not red tape
body:
| | Peter – Rural schools are already stretched thin, and without immediate action, the situation will get much worse. That’s why Marie has been unrelentingly fighting for solutions and calling attention to this critical issue.

Last week, Marie's bipartisan legislation to reauthorize the Secure Rural Schools (SRS) program passed the House – a long overdue win for rural communities in Southwest Washington and nationwide.

**Let's recognize this hard-earned progress and prove that a majority of Americans support investments in the equality of our children’s opportunities, making sure rural schools aren’t left behind.** [If you agree that where your kids live shouldn’t determine the quality of education they receive, add your name here:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342560/589136403/-391731991?refcode=MGP_EM_PT_180DO_251215_1_2&refcode2=071d0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNzFkMGE2ZC1mOWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jt5qw01dg5NdFryIYf6noMpbp1ge-8E0m8VHvfE9mm0=&emci=ff1c0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=071d0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=31138575)
| **[SUPPORT RURAL SCHOOLS](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342561/589136404/-391731991?refcode=MGP_EM_PT_180DO_251215_1_2&refcode2=071d0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNzFkMGE2ZC1mOWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jt5qw01dg5NdFryIYf6noMpbp1ge-8E0m8VHvfE9mm0=&emci=ff1c0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=071d0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=31138575)**
---

A decline in federal funding has already forced school districts in rural Southwest Washington to combine schools and lay off staff – and if they lose funding entirely, it would mean further layoffs, inability to repair aging facilities, and even cutting down to a four-day school week.

**It's past time to reauthorize the federal funding program upon which these schools rely, and now Marie's hard work to bring those dollars home is paying off.**

[Our children deserve access to an education that provides opportunities and helps prepare for their futures, regardless of zip code. If you agree, add your name here:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342562/589136405/-391731991?refcode=MGP_EM_PT_180DO_251215_1_2&refcode2=071d0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNzFkMGE2ZC1mOWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jt5qw01dg5NdFryIYf6noMpbp1ge-8E0m8VHvfE9mm0=&emci=ff1c0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=071d0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=31138575)
**[SUPPORT RURAL SCHOOLS](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342563/589136406/-391731991?refcode=MGP_EM_PT_180DO_251215_1_2&refcode2=071d0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNzFkMGE2ZC1mOWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jt5qw01dg5NdFryIYf6noMpbp1ge-8E0m8VHvfE9mm0=&emci=ff1c0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=071d0a6d-f9d9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=31138575)**

name: In This Together PAC
subject: Andy's showing Democrats how to win again.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
WSJ: Common sense wins, and Andy's taking it national.
Peter,

The _Wall Street Journal_ just published a profile on Gov. Andy Beshear — and honestly, it captured exactly why so many Democrats are rallying behind his leadership right now.

Andy isn't loud, he isn't flashy, and he isn't chasing headlines or picking political fights for attention. He talks like a real human being, stays laser-focused on what matters to families, and actually gets things done, even in deep-red territory.

While too many Democrats get tied up in "advocacy speak," Andy cuts straight to the basics — _**hunger instead of "food insecurity," addiction instead of "substance-use disorder."**_ It sounds small, but it's not. It's the difference between connecting with voters and losing them, and it makes all the difference for voters who just want confirmation that they're being heard.

He's won three statewide races in a place Donald Trump carried by 30 points, and maintains a 64% approval rating in Kentucky, the highest of any Democratic governor in the country. And he's done it by refusing to get dragged into Washington's daily chaos. While D.C. is stuck in endless noise and theatrics, Andy is showing the success that comes from leading with common sense and for the common good.

In This Together PAC and his DGA role are now critical engines for electing the kind of leaders our party desperately needs more of, **leaders who put people first and politics second.** We must do everything we can to support his work, today and all the way to Election Day next November.

[**If you want more Democrats like Andy, and are ready for a party that remembers what real, human leadership looks like, we need you with us right now. Chip in $5, or whatever you can, to support Andy's work and help elect commonsense, people-first leaders nationwide. clean_body: WSJ: Common sense wins, and Andy's taking it national. Peter, The Wall Street Journal just published a profile on Gov. Andy Beshear — and honestly, it captured exactly why so many Democrats are rallying behind his leadership right now. Andy isn't loud, he isn't flashy, and he isn't chasing headlines or picking political fights for attention. He talks like a real human being, stays laser-focused on what matters to families, and actually gets things done, even in deep-red territory. While too many Democrats get tied up in "advocacy speak," Andy cuts straight to the basics — hunger instead of "food insecurity," addiction instead of "substance-use disorder." It sounds small, but it's not. It's the difference between connecting with voters and losing them, and it makes all the difference for voters who just want confirmation that they're being heard. He's won three statewide races in a place Donald Trump carried by 30 points, and maintains a 64% approval rating in Kentucky, the highest of any Democratic governor in the country. And he's done it by refusing to get dragged into Washington's daily chaos. While D.C. is stuck in endless noise and theatrics, Andy is showing the success that comes from leading with common sense and for the common good. In This Together PAC and his DGA role are now critical engines for electing the kind of leaders our party desperately needs more of, leaders who put people first and politics second. We must do everything we can to support his work, today and all the way to Election Day next November. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, In This Together PAC | Andy and In This Together PAC are working to support leaders who will lead with compassion and decency, reject anger politics, and always do what's right. Will you donate today to support In This Together's mission? = _ Paid for by In This Together PAC. InThisTogetherPAC.com. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Robert Garcia
subject: House Democrats release more photos from Jeffrey Epstein’s estate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Robert Garcia for Congress
body:
Truth, accountability, and transparency still matter
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342777/589137310/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MzdlOTdiMy1mYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6Dhi3Qfuzt-3roIpeI9d1HvtLqh7S6CsgYM18HYUAEw=)
Oversight and accountability matters — **especially when it’s uncomfortable and powerful people would rather it go away.**
That’s why Democrats on the House Oversight Committee just released another batch of photos from Jeffrey Epstein’s estate. The images raise serious questions about Epstein’s relationships with some of the most powerful men in the world — **and about what Donald Trump is still hiding from the American people.**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342780/589137313/789508428?refcode=251215_RG_epstein-photos-fr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MzdlOTdiMy1mYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6Dhi3Qfuzt-3roIpeI9d1HvtLqh7S6CsgYM18HYUAEw=)
Let me be clear: This is about ending a cover-up and delivering justice for survivors.
For months, our committee has demanded transparency from the Department of Justice about the files it holds related to Jeffrey Epstein. Congress passed a resolution requiring those records to be released. We set deadlines. We asked basic questions. **And still, the answers haven’t come.**
**[That silence is unacceptable. And I need your help to keep up the fight in the face of relentless attacks from Donald Trump, Pam Bondi, and the entire administration. Can I count on you to step up with $5, $10, or more to send Trump a message that our grassroots movement for truth, accountability, and transparency isn’t going anywhere?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342787/589137316/789508428?refcode=251215_RG_epstein-photos-fr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MzdlOTdiMy1mYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6Dhi3Qfuzt-3roIpeI9d1HvtLqh7S6CsgYM18HYUAEw=)**
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342790/589137323/-309737922?refcode=251215_RG_epstein-photos-fr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MzdlOTdiMy1mYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6Dhi3Qfuzt-3roIpeI9d1HvtLqh7S6CsgYM18HYUAEw=)
Trust in our institutions depends on sunlight. When the government withholds information about crimes that involve wealth, power, and exploitation, it sends a dangerous message — that accountability only applies to some people.
I don't believe that. And neither should you.
I ran for Congress because I believe oversight is one of the most important responsibilities we have. Not just when it’s easy — but especially when it’s hard. **Especially when it involves presidents, billionaires, and insiders who think the rules don’t apply to them.**
That’s why we’re pushing the Department of Justice to release every file and refusing to let this story fade quietly into the background.
**[But standing up to this kind of pressure doesn’t happen on its own. The louder we get, the more resistance we face. And the only way we keep going is with the support of people who believe accountability still matters. So please, chip in whatever you can spare and let’s keep fighting for the truth.>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342792/589137325/789508428?refcode=251215_RG_epstein-photos-fr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MzdlOTdiMy1mYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6Dhi3Qfuzt-3roIpeI9d1HvtLqh7S6CsgYM18HYUAEw=)**
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342794/589137327/-309737922?refcode=251215_RG_epstein-photos-fr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MzdlOTdiMy1mYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6Dhi3Qfuzt-3roIpeI9d1HvtLqh7S6CsgYM18HYUAEw=)
Thank you,
Robert Garcia
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121342795/589137330/-309737922?refcode=251215_RG_epstein-photos-fr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MzdlOTdiMy1mYWQ5LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6Dhi3Qfuzt-3roIpeI9d1HvtLqh7S6CsgYM18HYUAEw=)

name: Justice Democrats
subject: Have you seen all the buzz about our latest endorsement?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Even Fox News is taking notice of our movement after our latest 2026
Democratic primary endorsement,
Peter.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251215-na.jJ8H8k&refcode=em20251215-37280&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37280%2E1203867%2EJnycPo ]Fox News: Progressive powerhouses launch primary war against Democratic
establishment ahead of 2026 elections

Justice Democrats is proudly backing Nida Allam in her rematch against
corporate Democrat Rep. Valerie Foushee in North Carolina’s 4th District.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251215-na.jJ8H8k&refcode=em20251215-37280&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=37280%2E1203867%2EJnycPo ]The Palestine Project: Growing dissatisfaction with the Israel lobby
may pave a lane for Nida Allam, who launched her congressional campaign in
North Carolina Thursday with the backing of Justice Democrats.

On Day 1, we joined Senator Bernie Sanders, Leaders We Deserve, Sunrise
Movement, Working Families Party, and Indian American Impact Fund to
support this campaign, because the progressive movement is ready to take
our power back from the billionaires and corporate lobbies raising all of
our prices.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251215-na.jJ8H8k&refcode=em20251215-37280&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1004&akid=37280%2E1203867%2EJnycPo ]David Hogg: Proud to join Bernie Sanders, Justice Democrats, Sunrise
Movement, Working Families Party, and Indian American Impact Fund to
endorse Nida Allam in her race for NC-04. We need more members of Congress
like Nida, who know that strongly worded letters, silence, and deference
to the status quo won’t save us.

This seat was bought by AIPAC and Crypto and maintained by Big Pharma, Big
Oil, and Wall Street. Not anymore — Nida is running to bring Medicare for
All and a corporate-free democracy to NC, and her campaign isn’t taking a
cent from AIPAC, crypto megadonors, or corporate PACs.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251215-na.jJ8H8k&refcode=em20251215-37280&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1005&akid=37280%2E1203867%2EJnycPo ]AIPAC Tracker: It’s a beautiful day in North Carolina! Nida Allam is
running for Congress in NC-04 and she’s not taking a penny from AIPAC and
the Israel lobby OR big oil and the fossil fuel lobby!

In 2022, this was the most expensive congressional primary in North
Carolina’s history. Now, if enough people put our resources together, we
can WIN this time around and give NC-04 the progressive, working-class
champion they deserve.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251215-na.jJ8H8k&refcode=em20251215-37280&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1006&akid=37280%2E1203867%2EJnycPo ]Ryan Grim: AIPAC and crypto PACs spent many millions to support Valerie
Foushee over Nida Allam in 2022 and Allam lost a very close race. She’s
now challenging her again in a district drawn even more favorably to
Allam.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251215-na.jJ8H8k&refcode=em20251215-37280&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1007&akid=37280%2E1203867%2EJnycPo ]Let’s show the establishment what a real grassroots movement looks
like. Split a contribution of $5 or whatever you can to
Justice Democrats and Nida’s campaign to help her defeat her corporate
opponent just three months from now!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251215-na.jJ8H8k&refcode=em20251215-37280&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1026&akid=37280%2E1203867%2EJnycPo ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251215-37280-footer&t=1027&refcode2=37280_1203867_JnycPo&akid=37280%2E1203867%2EJnycPo

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1028&akid=37280%2E1203867%2EJnycPo

#
Friend, Today marks the halfway point of the month – and just about two weeks until our end-of-quarter FEC deadline. We don’t love being this blunt, but we’re falling short of where we need to be to hit our goal. **Here are two reasons why hitting this mark is so important:**
* Experts from the _Cook Political Report_ have coined MI-08 as the “tipping point” – meaning this district will decide who takes control of the gavel next year.

* Even before this was announced, Republicans had already spent nearly a million dollars in attack ads against Kristen. Now, the attacks are only going to intensify as we get closer to Election Day.

**Remember: This seat went to Donald Trump by 2%,** and that razor-thin margin is exactly why national Republicans are targeting us so aggressively. [**$3 from you would go a long way in helping us fight back. Can we count on you to rush in a donation now? People are ready for something new -- not noise, not stunts, but real leadership grounded in respect, service, and accountability. **_No one expected we 'd have a real shot to change the direction of this district._** _Not the pundits._ _Not the establishment._ _Certainly not the GOP._ But here we are -- because the people of Northwest Georgia are tired of being a national punchline. We're tired of being divided. And we are ready to build something better. **I 'm running for Congress because this district deserves a representative who listens, who leads with integrity, and who understands that people come before politics. **And for the first time in years, we have a path to win -- but only if we build the resources to compete against the Republican machine gearing up to keep this seat red. **_Republicans are already pouring in cash. They know this seat can flip -- and they're terrified of losing the megaphone Marjorie Taylor Greene gave them._** [**I need you with me. Grassroots support is how we show the country that this district is ready for a new chapter. name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: I’ll make this quick
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=5150%2E35263%2Ej5bCXw)
To tell you the truth, I hate these emails as much as you do. But right now, I really have no choice — I had to reach out to you one last time before tonight's mid-December fundraising deadline. With so much going on in our country right now, I have spent very little time worrying about our fundraising so that I can focus on fighting Trump’s agenda. We’re halfway through the most important month of the year for my campaign -- and I’d like a minute to explain why I’m reaching out. **[If you’re short on time, $3 or any amount will be immediately put to work defending this seat.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121343356/589139216/-1516755731?refcode=em-12.15.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMzBiOTZkM2EtZmRkOS1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=2C8ldUQKhkRJYd_gf4AsSkJVyOL3jnQ1_C8pLUHBpas=)** After flipping CA-45 by 653 votes, I’m now one of the top GOP targets in the country. **Outside spending is already ramping up, and in recent days, there’s been chatter that a well-known Republican insider may be eyeing this race as a last-ditch effort to protect the slim House majority.** That’s in addition to the four declared GOP challengers already in this race. It’s not an exaggeration to say that I’m locked in the most competitive House race in California. _It’s a sign of just how high the stakes are._ Our final FEC deadline of the year was already a huge test. The stakes just got higher ‐‐ if we fall short, the GOP will double down on their crusade to flip this seat right back to red. But if we meet our goal, we send a clear message of strength. That’s why your grassroots support -- right now -- matters so much. **[If a deeply entrenched GOP figure jumps into this race, we need to be ready on day one with the resources to fight back. name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: The Squad doesn’t wait for permission →
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20251215-1720-foote&t=1001&akid=1720%2E342411%2EpsoHg4 ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

The Squad doesn’t wait for permission. We don’t ask corporations,
billionaires, or special interests to fund us. Our power comes from people
like you.

Right now, your support is what keeps our champions in Congress fighting
for justice, equity, and progressive change. Every dollar you give fuels
grassroots organizing and helps the Squad push back against the forces
trying to keep our country stuck in the past.

We’re halfway through the month, and we’re counting on you. Momentum isn’t
a luxury — it’s our lifeline. Without it, we risk giving the opposition
even a single inch. And we’re not giving them anything.

Here’s where you stand for the month:

Supporter Record for
Peter
Email: dpwillis67@gmail.com
December Donor: No — not
yet!
2025 Total:
$0
Suggested donation: $3
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251215-movement.SqJvC0&refcode=em20251215-1720&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1720%2E342411%2EpsoHg4 ]Contribute $3

*This data reflects donations made online with this email address. If
you've made a previous contribution via check or with a different email
address, it may not be reflected above. Thank you for supporting our
Squad.

When you pitch in, your contribution doesn’t just add up, it multiplies
our impact. It makes sure the Squad can keep challenging the status quo,
taking on the entrenched powers in Congress, and fighting for the policies
that actually put people first.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251215-movement.SqJvC0&refcode=em20251215-1720&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=1720%2E342411%2EpsoHg4 ]So we’re asking: will you chip in $3 ahead of tonight’s mid-month
deadline to keep our movement strong?

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20251215-movement.SqJvC0&refcode=em20251215-1720&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1022&akid=1720%2E342411%2EpsoHg4 ]Contribute ››

The opposition is relentless. So are we. And with you helping power this
grassroots movement, there’s nothing they can stop.

In solidarity,

The Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1720%2E342411%2EpsoHg4

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

clean_body: Hey, it’s Kirsten. I checked in with my Finance team this morning because we’re now less than two weeks from the most important public fundraising deadline we’ve faced this year. And when I looked at your supporter profile, I saw this: = Before I go any further, I have to ask: = | | = = This is our golden opportunity to prove to every GOP opponent watching our fundraising numbers — and the entire MAGA machine — that Democrats have the momentum to sweep the midterm elections. They’re coming for us with millions in MAGA money if we show ANY sign of weakness. We’re aiming to raise $100,000 before this public deadline hits. Grassroots supporters like you are the reason we hit goals like these, and we can’t afford to let this one slip. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = | = | = | = — Kirsten Gillibrand * A message from DSCC Chair Kirsten Gillibrand: =

name: Mark Kelly
subject: You deserve an explanation
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
December is an official FEC deadline month. What that means is, when it’s over, we are legally required to report how much money we raised AND the number of donations we received online will also be public.
#

Team —
I’ll get right to it: At the end of this email, I’m going to ask you for a donation.
But before I do, let me explain why. I know I owe you that much.
So, here’s the truth:
December is an official FEC deadline month. What that means is, when it’s over, we are legally required to report how much money we raised AND the number of donations we received online will also be public.
Everyone will be watching — the press, our opponents, and our allies as well. They all want to know if we can put together the kind of team we’ll need to face off against Trump and the entire executive branch.
Today, the 15th, is the midpoint to that FEC deadline. What we raise today will determine whether we’re in line to hit our goals, and we can’t fall behind. We have to show strength, because that’s the only thing people like Trump understand.
And that is why I am humbly asking:
[**Can you please make a $3 contribution to my campaign? We need an absolutely massive number of donations before we’re legally required to report out our numbers.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-dec25/.s2Rl9G&refcode=em251215-2-1mil&t=1&akid=6295%2E6688874%2EilrOXx)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Enough Is Enough—Tell Congress to End Obamacare’s Waste
body:
Billions wasted, fraud ignored, families stuck with higher costs—act now.
[](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org/1/0100019b23ffaea5-ebf9bcc9-8b77-45c4-bcb1-8d74ef54ca82-000000/OchkDl-B194PNzrY3ZYhDCszHwYFhYp6wPDnl0BdNFo=435)Patriot–Obamacare is a failure—plain and simple.Washington promised that Obamacare would lower costs and expand access to quality care. Instead, families are facing higher premiums, fewer choices, and coverage that often falls apart when it’s needed most.Rather than admit failure, Congress doubled down during COVID—dumping hundreds of billions of taxpayer dollars into expanded subsidies to conceal Obamacare’s soaring insurance costs.The result? [_**Massive waste and abuse.**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Faction.teapartypatriots.org%2Fpetition%2Fno-to-obamacare-covid-subsidies%2F/1/0100019b23ffaea5-ebf9bcc9-8b77-45c4-bcb1-8d74ef54ca82-000000/NigVRixhJWC5NpDkZL15jLi2YjBX9lz6OU1lOKRRIdM=435) At least 160,000 Obamacare applications were altered without authorization by insurance brokers or agents, and fraud continues to plague the system. Meanwhile, more than 1.5 million households earning **400% of the Federal Poverty Level** received taxpayer subsidies. Even worse, families making **up to $600,000 a year** are now receiving government-funded health insurance.Insurance companies made out like bandits—collecting hundreds of billions in taxpayer money while raising premiums, restricting care, and driving costs even higher.Now Democrats—and disturbingly, several Republicans—want to make these so-called “temporary” COVID-era subsidies permanent. That’s a stunning reversal from lawmakers who once promised to repeal Obamacare entirely.After spending **$1.3 trillion over ten years** , Obamacare is still unaffordable. Throwing another half-trillion dollars at a broken system won’t fix it—it will only deepen our debt and reward failure.**_It’s time for Congress to stop the waste._**[_**_Call your Member of Congress now_**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Faction.teapartypatriots.org%2Fpetition%2Fno-to-obamacare-covid-subsidies%2F/2/0100019b23ffaea5-ebf9bcc9-8b77-45c4-bcb1-8d74ef54ca82-000000/yGr6qMgmEvMEcuSrLI7YFifgcIA45esLQTDnNNgWwho=435) and demand an end to the COVID-era Obamacare subsidies.[TAKE ACTION: CONTACT CONGRESS](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Faction.teapartypatriots.org%2Fpetition%2Fno-to-obamacare-covid-subsidies%2F/3/0100019b23ffaea5-ebf9bcc9-8b77-45c4-bcb1-8d74ef54ca82-000000/7o0IZTjo1pieJrKe_pae2eNxJDKie5daIIrViaLuJ7w=435)Enough is enough.In liberty,Jenny Beth Martin[CONTRIBUTE](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Fdonate%2F/1/0100019b23ffaea5-ebf9bcc9-8b77-45c4-bcb1-8d74ef54ca82-000000/Kr3f_2n7lFDPNMXCZbTnd1U10QAVpYcUZg6c3y3hNl0=435) _This email list is a joint venture of the following separate organizations: Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc., Tea Party Patriots Foundation, and Tea Party Patriots Citizens Fund. All rights reserved.__Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. operates as a social welfare organization under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. Contributions to Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. are not deductible as charitable contributions for income tax purposes._ To donate by mail, send your check made payable to:[_**Tea Party Patriots Action**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Fdonate%2F/2/0100019b23ffaea5-ebf9bcc9-8b77-45c4-bcb1-8d74ef54ca82-000000/ClHz1d0TvOV-SHOz0nINxRSPfvs3FibKgE2tXbZyU9g=435)

clean_body: Billions wasted, fraud ignored, families stuck with higher costs—act now. –Obamacare is a failure—plain and simple.Washington promised that Obamacare would lower costs and expand access to quality care. Instead, families are facing higher premiums, fewer choices, and coverage that often falls apart when it’s needed most.Rather than admit failure, Congress doubled down during COVID—dumping hundreds of billions of taxpayer dollars into expanded subsidies to conceal Obamacare’s soaring insurance costs.The result? At least 160,000 Obamacare applications were altered without authorization by insurance brokers or agents, and fraud continues to plague the system. Meanwhile, more than 1.5 million households earning 400% of the Federal Poverty Level received taxpayer subsidies. Even worse, families making up to $600,000 a year are now receiving government-funded health insurance.Insurance companies made out like bandits—collecting hundreds of billions in taxpayer money while raising premiums, restricting care, and driving costs even higher.Now Democrats—and disturbingly, several Republicans—want to make these so-called “temporary” COVID-era subsidies permanent. That’s a stunning reversal from lawmakers who once promised to repeal Obamacare entirely.After spending $1.3 trillion over ten years , Obamacare is still unaffordable. Throwing another half-trillion dollars at a broken system won’t fix it—it will only deepen our debt and reward failure.It’s time for Congress to stop the waste. and demand an end to the COVID-era Obamacare subsidies. is enough.In liberty,Jenny Beth Martin This email list is a joint venture of the following separate organizations: Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc., Tea Party Patriots Foundation, and Tea Party Patriots Citizens Fund. body:
A bipartisan group of lawmakers just voted down the Trump gerrymandering scheme.
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121344204/589141448/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImZlZDZmOWZjLWZmZDktZjAxMS04MTk2LTAwMGQzYTFkNWFmZSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=pMXZLpFV84RZUCKNL0WsF6ocG403rTlzdajbk6MuoXM=&emci=57fd4b1e-ead9-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=fed6f9fc-ffd9-f011-8196-000d3a1d5afe&ceid=8431045)

Peter, What happened in Indiana just sent shockwaves through the political world. A bipartisan supermajority — including more than half of Republicans — in the Indiana Senate locked arms to reject a Trump-led extreme gerrymander. The redistricting fight in Indiana, for now, is over, and democracy prevailed. These lawmakers stood strong not just in the face of bomb threats and their homes being swatted — but also after President Trump reportedly said that “all federal funding will be stripped from the state” if his extreme map didn’t pass. This outcome didn’t happen out of nowhere. It’s what happens when we fight back against anti-democracy schemes designed to take power away from voters. Hoosiers got mad, they got organized, and they won. [**We need to replicate this playbook in the states getting ready to dive into this redistricting crisis — will you rush in a donation to help the NDRC build the political power to defeat Republican power grabs in Florida, Kansas, and anywhere else they try to rig the midterm elections?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121344205/589141449/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_IndianaMap_email_20251215-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaMap_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251215-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaMap_&utm_campaign=IndianaMap&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImZlZDZmOWZjLWZmZDktZjAxMS04MTk2LTAwMGQzYTFkNWFmZSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=pMXZLpFV84RZUCKNL0WsF6ocG403rTlzdajbk6MuoXM=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

For the first time since Texas plunged the nation into a crisis by redrawing its congressional map at the request of the Trump White House, we’re seeing real cracks emerge in the Republican coalition. Don’t take it from us — _USA Today_ called this Indiana vote a “stunning blow to President Donald Trump's hotly contested redistricting campaign.”
**Our job now is to ensure that this victory for democracy becomes a turning point in our fight for fairness. **
We want states that are on the fence about pursuing their own mid-decade redraw to see how this played out and decide it’s not worth the risk. We want Republican lawmakers across the country who are thinking about following Missouri, Texas, and North Carolina’s lead to remember how Indiana voters mobilized and demanded lawmakers reject this power grab.
But that means we have a lot more work to do. This redistricting crisis is bound to get worse in the weeks ahead, with states like Florida eager to enter the fray.
**Here’s the thing: Now we know that when we fight back, we can defeat them.[Make a donation today, and let’s fight even harder to ensure the Trump power grab fails miserably in 2026.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121344227/589141456/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_IndianaMap_email_20251215-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaMap_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251215-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaMap_&utm_campaign=IndianaMap&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImZlZDZmOWZjLWZmZDktZjAxMS04MTk2LTAwMGQzYTFkNWFmZSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=pMXZLpFV84RZUCKNL0WsF6ocG403rTlzdajbk6MuoXM=)**

name: Sen. Tom Cotton
subject: There’s no path to a Senate Majority without Arkansas

It’s been a big year.

name: Heidi Hall
subject: Our rural communities deserve better
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HEIDI HALL FOR CONGRESS
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251215_em_fr_b1_HH_standard [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251215_em_fr_b1_HH_standard]
Friend,
I moved to Grass Valley from the Bay Area 20 years ago because I was looking for a place where I could settle down and raise my two boys as a single mother.
What I found was community, hardworking people, and a place I'm proud to call home.
But I also found that we had no real representation in our government. Our needs seemingly didn't matter to Sacramento or Washington, and I watched as year after year the same issues we begged our elected leaders to fix went unaddressed.
We were always being placed on the back burner, and it was like everyone was used to it.
That's why I ran for local office and focused on fighting tooth and nail to deliver results on the issues that truly mattered— winning my race without a runoff and flipping a purple seat.
Now, as billionaires and corporations buy access to our politicians and the two sitting congressmen in this race (neither of whom actually live in this district) continue to put working people on the backburner, I'm running to make a difference.
Our district needs Medicare for All, it needs a plan to address climate change, and it needs a leader who actually cares.
Friend, will you help me fight for our communities in Congress and help me flip this seat by donating any amount that you can to our grassroots campaign today? We need your support before our final End of Quarter Deadline of the year. Can you chip in now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251215_em_fr_b1_HH_standard]
If you have saved your information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process instantly.DONATE $15 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/Hall-email?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=20251215_em_fr_b1_HH_standard_]

Thank you,
Heidi Hall
Heidi Hall has spent her three-decade public service career working with others to solve problems, protect our land and water, improve health, and build opportunities for others, all while raising two boys primarily as a single mom.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251215_em_fr_b1_HH_standard [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20251215_em_fr_b1_HH_standard]
Raised by parents who had persisted to make a middle-class life for their kids in Contra Costa County while lifting up others, Heidi was taught to push through and overcome challenges rather than complain about them. She learned to stick up for the vulnerable, to respect working people, and to value equal opportunity and justice.
After graduating from Pomona College and earning graduate degrees from Columbia and Rutgers, Heidi went to work for the Rockefeller Foundation promoting reproductive care and then began her career at the Environmental Protection Agency, stopping the dumping of toxic waste and holding corporate polluters accountable.
Today, Heidi chairs the Nevada County Board of Supervisors, where she’s taken on homelessness, expanded broadband access, and worked to reduce crime and illegal cannabis growth. Heidi flipped her seat from red to blue – and now she’s ready to do it again in Congress to take on toxic politics in Washington and put people first.
Prefer to donate via mail? Address a check to:
Heidi Hall for Congress
PO Box 729
Grass Valley, CA 95945

clean_body: [] Friend, I moved to Grass Valley from the Bay Area 20 years ago because I was looking for a place where I could settle down and raise my two boys as a single mother. What I found was community, hardworking people, and a place I'm proud to call home. But I also found that we had no real representation in our government. Our needs seemingly didn't matter to Sacramento or Washington, and I watched as year after year the same issues we begged our elected leaders to fix went unaddressed. We were always being placed on the back burner, and it was like everyone was used to it. That's why I ran for local office and focused on fighting tooth and nail to deliver results on the issues that truly mattered— winning my race without a runoff and flipping a purple seat. Now, as billionaires and corporations buy access to our politicians and the two sitting congressmen in this race (neither of whom actually live in this district) continue to put working people on the backburner, I'm running to make a difference. Our district needs Medicare for All, it needs a plan to address climate change, and it needs a leader who actually cares. Friend, will you help me fight for our communities in Congress and help me flip this seat by donating any amount that you can to our grassroots campaign today? We need your support before our final End of Quarter Deadline of the year. Can you chip in now? [] If you have saved your information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process instantly.DONATE $15 [] DONATE $35 [] DONATE $50 [] DONATE $100 [] DONATE $250 [] Another amount [] Thank you, Heidi Hall Heidi Hall has spent her three-decade public service career working with others to solve problems, protect our land and water, improve health, and build opportunities for others, all while raising two boys primarily as a single mom. [] Raised by parents who had persisted to make a middle-class life for their kids in Contra Costa County while lifting up others, Heidi was taught to push through and overcome challenges rather than complain about them. She learned to stick up for the vulnerable, to respect working people, and to value equal opportunity and justice. After graduating from Pomona College and earning graduate degrees from Columbia and Rutgers, Heidi went to work for the Rockefeller Foundation promoting reproductive care and then began her career at the Environmental Protection Agency, stopping the dumping of toxic waste and holding corporate polluters accountable. Today, Heidi chairs the Nevada County Board of Supervisors, where she’s taken on homelessness, expanded broadband access, and worked to reduce crime and illegal cannabis growth. Heidi flipped her seat from red to blue – and now she’s ready to do it again in Congress to take on toxic politics in Washington and put people first. Prefer to donate via mail? Address a check to: Heidi Hall for Congress PO Box 729 Grass Valley, CA 95945 SUPPORT HEIDI [] PAID FOR BY HEIDI HALL FOR CONGRESS Heidi Hall for Congress PO Box 729 Grass Valley, CA 95945 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

subject: About the rabid dog thing…

**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121344975/589144264/-358924572?amount=7&refcode=DOTL_20251115_EMSPL_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIxZTYxMDY5LTAxZGEtZjAxMS04MTk2LTAwMGQzYTFkNWFmZSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WDNIXqOETIHbY-k1eDsDKm4yIeLxDlatoOfm6Lkvym4=)** My cousin RFK Jr. is a rabid dog, barking for Trump. The White House is calling me ridiculous for saying so, but they're wrong. Trump has my cousin spewing dangerous lies about vaccines, about Americans' health, and about science.

This is about the far-right agenda, _not_ "making America healthy again," and I'm not afraid to say so.

The rest of Trump's cabinet is no better, either. They're all on his leash.

Congress _should_ be a critical line of defense against Trump's radical agenda, but for checks and balances to work, we need leaders who are willing to stand up.

I promise I won't let any of his authoritarianism, lies, or corruption go unchecked.

Because of this long-term investment, EMILYs List members have been central to the success of Democratic pro-choice women in tough races up and down the ballot. That's why, nearly four decades later, our annual members remain at the forefront of our strategy and victories every election cycle. And once you've made your voice heard, please consider supporting our work by activating your 2026 EMILYs List membership. name: Senator Mark Warner
subject: My friend, Elaine Luria:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
Elaine Luria won’t rest until Virginians are safe, secure, and free.

|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121345099/589144401/-2010479286?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_LuriaSplit_251215_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTI4ZjM3Zi0wMWRhLWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDVhZmUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_CGDZqSZ5BVMzsDzTGl6yuavH31HhrxYKu4s-vmC_6c=) Virginia means everything to me, Derek.

It took me in, allowed me to call it home, and I’ve spent decades trying to give back as much as it’s given me, creating jobs, serving as Governor, and now, serving as Senator.

Virginians are good, decent, patriotic people. I know they deserve leaders who reflect those qualities. **I know my friend, Congresswoman Elaine Luria, is a leader of that caliber.**

The people of Hampton Roads and the Eastern Shore will have a true champion in Elaine Luria. We have so much left to accomplish together on Capitol Hill, but before we can get to work, **she’s tasked with winning back one of the most competitive House districts in the country.

[_Will you help her tackle this challenge by splitting a contribution between Elaine Luria’s campaign and mine today? Your support will help us win back the House and Senate majorities and restore moral leadership to America._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121345100/589144403/-2010479286?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_LuriaSplit_251215_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTI4ZjM3Zi0wMWRhLWYwMTEtODE5Ni0wMDBkM2ExZDVhZmUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_CGDZqSZ5BVMzsDzTGl6yuavH31HhrxYKu4s-vmC_6c=)**
